Reading Mode
This page keeps the whole session intact while making long transcripts easier to scan.
  • Messages stay in left/right conversation bubbles.
  • Tool calls and results are folded by default.
  • Reasoning steps remain visible without leaking encrypted content.
  • Context, task state, and token counters stay in the timeline for auditability.
192 dialog 701 tool traffic
Conversation Flow
Use the controls to focus on one slice of the session: human prompts, assistant output, tool traffic, reasoning markers, or system context. The raw structure is preserved without forcing everything into one flat blob.

Implement the following plan:

# Phase 1 执行计划: Transcript 核心模块

## Context

Phase 0 已完成（commit aa95327），TS 骨架 + 工具链就绪。Phase 1 将 5 个 Python transcript 模块（共 ~1,068 行）移植为 TypeScript，并编写 ~90 个测试。这是整个迁移中最大的工作量（约 40%）。

## 依赖关系

types/transcript.ts  (已完成)
        |
        v
  transcript/common.ts  ← 必须最先实现
   /      |       \
  v       v        v
parse.ts  db.ts  discover.ts  ← 可并行
          \       /
           v     v
        import.ts  ← 最后实现（依赖以上全部）

## 执行步骤

### Step 1: src/transcript/common.ts + 测试

来源: scripts/_transcript_common.py (294 行) → ~250 TS 行
测试: tests/transcript/common.test.ts (~55 个测试)

实现 5 组函数：

1. 常量: CLIENTS, TEXT_BLOCK_TYPES, SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES, TOOL_USE_TYPES, TOOL_RESULT_TYPES（用 ReadonlySet<string>）

2. 通用工具 (12 个函数):
- slugify(value, fallback) — ASCII 过滤用 value.replace(/[^\x00-\x7F]/g, '')（等价 Python 的 encode("ascii","ignore")）
- sha256File(filePath) — node:crypto + readFileSync
- contentHash(text) — createHash('sha256').update(text,'utf8').digest('hex').slice(0,16)
- normalizeTimestamp(value, fallback) — 处理 number（Unix 秒 <1e12）/string/Date → YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ
- earliestTimestamp, parseIsoTimestamp, timestampPartition
- joinParagraphs, summarizeValue, truncate, yamlEscape

3. 数据访问 (5 个函数): ensureDict, ensureList, getNested, loadJsonl, loadJson

4. 文本提取 (5 个函数): extractTextBlocks, extractSimpleText, extractGeminiText, extractGeminiParts, findFirstMappingWithKeys

5. 去重 (2 个函数): deduplicateMessages（签名: timestamp+role+contentHash）, deduplicateToolEvents（签名: timestamp+contentHash）

### Step 2: src/transcript/parse.ts + 测试（与 Step 3a/3b 并行）

来源: scripts/_transcript_parse.py (361 行) → ~300 TS 行
测试: tests/transcript/parse.test.ts (新文件, ~20 个测试)

- inferClient(client, sourcePath) — path .includes() 检测
- parseTranscript(client, sourcePath) — 分发函数
- parseCodexTranscript(filePath) — JSONL, 7 种 payload_type
- parseClaudeTranscript(filePath) — JSONL, content blocks (text/tool_use/tool_result)
- parseGeminiTranscript(filePath) — JSON 或 JSONL, 递归 visitor, role 映射 model→assistant

测试用 mkdtempSync 创建临时 JSONL/JSON 文件。

### Step 3a: src/transcript/db.ts + 测试（与 Step 2/3b 并行）

来源: scripts/_transcript_index.py (100 行) → ~80 TS 行
测试: tests/transcript/db.test.ts (新文件, ~6 个测试)

- upsertSearchIndex(dbPath, manifest) — async（sql.js WASM 加载）
- 表 transcripts, 18 列, PK=(client, project, session_id, raw_sha256)
- PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL
- INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET（14 个数据列）
- sql.js: initSqlJs() 缓存, db.export() → writeFileSync

### Step 3b: src/transcript/discover.ts + 测试（与 Step 2/3a 并行）

来源: scripts/_transcript_discover.py (100 行) → ~90 TS 行
测试: tests/transcript/discover.test.ts (新文件, ~12 个测试)
依赖: 需添加 fast-glob 到 package.json dependencies（Node 20 无稳定 fs.globSync）

- defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot() — ~/.memorytree/transcripts
- defaultClientRoots() — { codex: ~/.codex, claude: ~/.claude, gemini: ~/.gemini }
- discoverSourceFiles(clients?) — glob 模式 per client, 去重 by resolved lowercase posix, mtime 降序
- inferProjectSlug(parsed) — cwd 名 / Claude 特殊处理（strip ^[a-z]--）/ gemini parent / fallback
- transcriptMatchesRepo, projectSlugsMatch, safeFileMtime

Glob 模式:
- codex: sessions/*/.jsonl
- claude: projects/*/.jsonl
- gemini: tmp//checkpoints//.json, .../.jsonl, history//, chats/*/

### Step 4: src/transcript/import.ts + 测试

来源: scripts/_transcript_import.py (213 行) → ~180 TS 行
测试: tests/transcript/import.test.ts (新文件, ~12 个测试)

- importTranscript(parsed, root, globalRoot, projectSlug, rawUploadPermission, mirrorToRepo) — async（调用 upsertSearchIndex）
- 路径: {session_label}__{sha256[:8]}.ext, year/month 分区
- 5 路径对: repo raw/clean/manifest, global raw/clean/manifest
- transcriptHasContent(parsed) — messages 或 tool_events 非空
- writeCleanMarkdown(parsed, manifest, filePath) — YAML frontmatter (13 字段 + yamlEscape) + body
- 幂等性: manifestSignature, manifestChanged, preserveExistingImportTimestamp
- 文件操作: writeJson (2-space indent + \n), appendJsonl (仅 changed 时追加), copyFile

### Step 5: src/transcript/index.ts — Barrel re-export

~30 行，re-export 所有公共 API。

### Step 6: 集成测试

文件: tests/transcript/integration.test.ts (新文件)

端到端: 创建临时目录 → 写入 JSONL → parse → import → 验证:
- raw 文件复制到 repo + global
- clean markdown 有正确 YAML frontmatter
- manifest JSON 写入
- SQLite 索引更新
- 重复导入幂等（preserved imported_at）

### Step 7: 验证 + 提交

npm run typecheck   # 0 errors
npm run lint        # 0 errors
npm test            # all passing, 80%+ coverage
npm run build       # dist/cli.js

## 新增依赖

| 包 | 类型 | 用途 |
|---|------|------|
| fast-glob | dependencies | discover.ts glob 文件扫描 |

## 文件清单

| 文件 | 操作 | 行数(估) |
|------|------|---------|
| src/transcript/common.ts | 重写 | ~250 |
| src/transcript/parse.ts | 重写 | ~300 |
| src/transcript/db.ts | 重写 | ~80 |
| src/transcript/discover.ts | 重写 | ~90 |
| src/transcript/import.ts | 重写 | ~180 |
| src/transcript/index.ts | 重写 | ~30 |
| tests/transcript/common.test.ts | 重写 | ~350 |
| tests/transcript/parse.test.ts | 新建 | ~250 |
| tests/transcript/db.test.ts | 新建 | ~100 |
| tests/transcript/discover.test.ts | 新建 | ~150 |
| tests/transcript/import.test.ts | 新建 | ~200 |
| tests/transcript/integration.test.ts | 新建 | ~80 |
| package.json | 编辑(+fast-glob) | minor |

总计: ~930 源码行 + ~1,130 测试行

## 关键风险

| 风险 | 应对 |
|------|------|
| normalizeTimestamp JS Date 精度差异 | 测试覆盖所有 Python 测试用例 + edge cases |
| slugify 非 ASCII 行为差异 | 测试 CJK/emoji, regex [^\x00-\x7F] 等价 Python encode("ascii","ignore") |
| sql.js WASM 在 vitest 中加载 | 配置 locateFile 指向 node_modules/sql.js/dist/ |
| Windows 路径分隔符 | 所有路径经 toPosixPath() 处理 |

## 验证方式

1. npm run typecheck — 0 errors
2. npm run lint — 0 errors
3. npm test — ~90 新测试全部通过
4. 覆盖率 ≥ 80%（transcript 模块）
5. npm run build 成功

If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\ai\.claude\projects\D--demo1-memorytree-workflow\5004f9e7-85a4-49a9-bbb0-07f26d068121.jsonl

[
  {
    "text": "Perfect! I have read all six Python transcript source files. Here's a comprehensive summary of the implementation details for TypeScript porting:\n\n## Complete Python Transcript Module Reference\n\n### 1. **_transcript_common.py** (294 lines)\n**Constants:**\n- `CLIENTS = {\"codex\", \"claude\", \"gemini\"}`\n- `TEXT_BLOCK_TYPES = {\"input_text\", \"output_text\", \"text\"}`\n- `SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES = {\"thinking\", \"reasoning\"}`\n- `TOOL_USE_TYPES = {\"tool_use\"}`\n- `TOOL_RESULT_TYPES = {\"tool_result\"}`\n\n**Type Signatures:**\n- `slugify(value: str, fallback: str = \"session\") -> str` — Converts text to lowercase ASCII slug with `-` separators\n- `sha256_file(path: Path) -> str` — Computes SHA256 of file in 1MB chunks\n- `normalize_timestamp(value: Any, fallback: str | None = None) -> str` — Normalizes timestamps (int/float/datetime/str) to ISO format with `Z` suffix\n- `earliest_timestamp(current: str, candidate: Any) -> str` — Returns earliest of two timestamps\n- `parse_iso_timestamp(value: str) -> datetime | None` — Parses ISO timestamp, handles `Z` suffix conversion to `+00:00`\n- `timestamp_partition(timestamp: str) -> tuple[str, str]` — Returns `(year, month)` pair from ISO timestamp\n- `join_paragraphs(parts: Any) -> str` — Joins parts with `\\n\\n`, filters empty strings\n- `summarize_value(value: Any, limit: int = 180) -> str` — JSON-serializes value with truncation\n- `truncate(text: str, limit: int) -> str` — Truncates with `...` suffix\n- `yaml_escape(value: str) -> str` — Escapes backslash, quotes, newlines for YAML; wraps in double quotes\n- `ensure_dict(value: Any) -> dict[str, Any]` — Coerces to dict or returns `{}`\n- `ensure_list(value: Any) -> list[Any]` — Coerces to list or returns `[]`\n- `get_nested(payload: dict[str, Any], *keys: str) -> Any` — Safe nested dict access\n- `load_jsonl(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, Any]]` — Loads JSONL (ignores decoding errors/blank lines)\n- `load_json(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any] | None` — Loads JSON file or returns None\n- `extract_text_blocks(blocks: Any) -> str` — Extracts text from block list, skipping thinking/tool types\n- `extract_simple_text(value: Any) -> str` — Recursively extracts text from nested structures\n- `extract_gemini_text(node: dict[str, Any]) -> str` — Extracts text from Gemini-format node\n- `extract_gemini_parts(value: Any) -> list[str]` — Recursively extracts text parts from Gemini structure\n- `find_first_mapping_with_keys(value: Any, keys: set[str]) -> dict[str, Any] | None` — Recursively finds first dict with matching keys\n- `_content_hash(text: str) -> str` — SHA256 prefix (16 chars)\n- `deduplicate_messages(messages: list[TranscriptMessage]) -> list[TranscriptMessage]` — Deduplicates by `(timestamp, role, content_hash)`\n- `deduplicate_tool_events(events: list[TranscriptToolEvent]) -> list[TranscriptToolEvent]` — Deduplicates by `(timestamp, summary_hash)`\n\n**Edge Cases:**\n- Timestamp normalization handles empty strings, malformed ISO, missing timezone\n- YAML escape converts `Z` to `+00:00` for fromisoformat compatibility\n- Text extraction filters empty blocks and non-dict items\n- Gemini text extraction checks multiple possible keys: `parts`, `content`, `text`, `message`\n\n---\n\n### 2. **_transcript_parse.py** (361 lines)\n**Dataclasses:**\n```python\n@dataclass\nclass TranscriptMessage:\n    role: str\n    text: str\n    timestamp: str | None = None\n\n@dataclass\nclass TranscriptToolEvent:\n    summary: str\n    timestamp: str | None = None\n\n@dataclass\nclass ParsedTranscript:\n    client: str\n    session_id: str\n    title: str\n    started_at: str\n    cwd: str\n    branch: str\n    messages: list[TranscriptMessage]\n    tool_events: list[TranscriptToolEvent]\n    source_path: Path\n```\n\n**Function Signatures:**\n- `infer_client(client: str, source: Path) -> str` — Auto-detects client from path patterns:\n  - `\"codex\"`: contains `/.codex/` or filename matches `rollout-*`\n  - `\"claude\"`: contains `/.claude/` or `/projects/`\n  - `\"gemini\"`: contains `/.gemini/` or filename contains `checkpoint`\n- `parse_transcript(client: str, source: Path) -> ParsedTranscript` — Dispatches to client-specific parser\n- `parse_codex_transcript(path: Path) -> ParsedTranscript` — JSONL parser for Codex format\n- `parse_claude_transcript(path: Path) -> ParsedTranscript` — JSONL parser for Claude format\n- `parse_gemini_transcript(path: Path) -> ParsedTranscript` — JSON/JSONL parser for Gemini format\n\n**Codex Parser Details:**\n- Reads JSONL records\n- `session_meta` records populate `session_id`, `title`, `started_at`, `cwd`, `branch`\n- `response_item` and `event_msg` records with `message` payload type extract text\n- `function_call`, `custom_tool_call`, `function_call_output`, `custom_tool_call_output` create tool events\n- Calls `deduplicate_messages()` and `deduplicate_tool_events()` before returning\n\n**Claude Parser Details:**\n- Reads JSONL records with fields: `type` (user/assistant), `message.role`, `message.content`, `timestamp`, `sessionId`, `cwd`, `gitBranch`\n- Handles content as string or array of blocks\n- Text blocks extracted from `type: \"text\"` blocks\n- Tool use/result blocks create tool events\n- Deduplication applied\n\n**Gemini Parser Details:**\n- Reads JSON or JSONL (auto-detected by suffix)\n- Recursive `visit()` function traverses entire structure\n- Looks for metadata: `sessionId`, `chatId`, `cwd`, `projectRoot`, `branch`, `timestamp`\n- Detects message roles: `user`, `assistant`, `model` (model → assistant)\n- Detects tool calls via: `toolUse.name`, `toolName`, `functionName`, `tool`\n- Deduplication applied\n\n**Edge Cases:**\n- Empty content fields yield no messages\n- Tool events always created even if timestamp is None\n- Gemini handles deeply nested structures via recursive traversal\n- Role normalization: Claude/Codex use explicit roles; Gemini standardizes `model` → `assistant`\n\n---\n\n### 3. **_transcript_index.py** (101 lines)\n**Function Signature:**\n- `upsert_search_index(db_path: Path, manifest: dict[str, Any]) -> None`\n\n**SQLite Operations:**\n- Creates table `transcripts` with columns:\n  - `client`, `project`, `session_id`, `raw_sha256`, `title`, `started_at`, `imported_at`\n  - `cwd`, `branch`, `raw_source_path`, `raw_upload_permission`\n  - `global_raw_path`, `global_clean_path`, `repo_raw_path`, `repo_clean_path`, `repo_manifest_path`\n  - `message_count`, `tool_event_count`\n  - PRIMARY KEY: `(client, project, session_id, raw_sha256)`\n- Uses `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT ... DO UPDATE` for upsert\n- Enables WAL mode\n- Takes manifest dict keys matching column names\n\n**Edge Cases:**\n- Schema created if not exists\n- Upsert updates all fields on conflict\n\n---\n\n### 4. **_transcript_discover.py** (101 lines)\n**Function Signatures:**\n- `default_global_transcript_root() -> Path` — Returns `~/.memorytree/transcripts`\n- `default_client_roots() -> dict[str, Path]` — Maps clients to home-relative paths:\n  - `codex`: `~/.codex`\n  - `claude`: `~/.claude`\n  - `gemini`: `~/.gemini`\n- `discover_source_files(clients: set[str] | None = None) -> list[tuple[str, Path]]` — Scans for transcripts:\n  - Codex: `sessions/**/*.jsonl`\n  - Claude: `projects/**/*.jsonl`\n  - Gemini: `tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.{json,jsonl}`, `history/**/*.{json,jsonl}`, `chats/**/*.{json,jsonl}`\n  - Returns tuples of `(client, path)` sorted by mtime descending\n  - Deduplicates by resolved lowercase path\n- `infer_project_slug(parsed: ParsedTranscript) -> str` — Derives project slug from:\n  1. CWD directory name (slugified)\n  2. Claude: parent directory name (strip `^[a-z]--` prefix, slugified)\n  3. Gemini: parent directory name (slugified)\n  4. Fallback: `\"unknown-project\"`\n- `transcript_matches_repo(parsed: ParsedTranscript, repo_root: Path, repo_slug: str) -> bool` — Checks if transcript matches repo:\n  - True if CWD == repo_root or repo_root is parent of CWD\n  - Or if project slugs match (and neither is \"unknown-project\")\n- `project_slugs_match(left: str, right: str) -> bool` — Both non-empty, equal, not \"unknown-project\"\n- `safe_file_mtime(path: Path) -> float` — Returns mtime or `0.0` on OSError\n\n**Edge Cases:**\n- Claude project slug strips leading `[a-z]--` (artifact of naming scheme)\n- Unknown projects never match\n- Glob patterns case-sensitive on Unix, case-insensitive on Windows\n\n---\n\n### 5. **_transcript_import.py** (214 lines)\n**Function Signatures:**\n- `import_transcript(parsed: ParsedTranscript, root: Path, global_root: Path, project_slug: str, raw_upload_permission: str, mirror_to_repo: bool = True) -> dict[str, Any]`\n  - Returns manifest dict with paths and metadata\n  - Creates directory structure with year/month partitioning\n  - Writes raw file, clean markdown, manifests, and updates SQLite index\n  - Creates/appends to global event log (sessions.jsonl)\n  - Preserves existing `imported_at` timestamp if content unchanged\n  - Deduplicates event log appends based on manifest signature\n- `transcript_has_content(parsed: ParsedTranscript) -> bool` — Returns True if messages or tool_events exist\n- `write_clean_markdown(parsed: ParsedTranscript, manifest: dict[str, Any], path: Path) -> None` — Writes frontmatter + sections:\n  - YAML frontmatter with metadata\n  - H1 title\n  - \"## Metadata\" section\n  - \"## Messages\" section (H3 per message with timestamp)\n  - \"## Tool Events\" section (bullet list with timestamps)\n- `preserve_existing_import_timestamp(existing: dict[str, Any] | None, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]` — Keeps old `imported_at` if signature unchanged\n- `manifest_signature(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]` — Returns copy excluding `imported_at`\n- `manifest_changed(path: Path, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> bool` — Compares new manifest to file\n- `write_json(path: Path, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None` — Writes JSON with newline suffix\n- `append_jsonl(path: Path, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None` — Appends single JSON line\n- `copy_file(source: Path, destination: Path) -> None` — Shallow copy with `shutil.copy2()`, skips if same path\n\n**Directory Structure:**\n```\nrepo_root/\n  Memory/\n    06_transcripts/\n      raw/{client}/{year}/{month}/{stem__{sha8}}{ext}\n      clean/{client}/{year}/{month}/{stem__{sha8}}.md\n      manifests/{client}/{year}/{month}/{stem__{sha8}}.json\n\nglobal_root/\n  raw/{client}/{project}/{year}/{month}/{stem__{sha8}}{ext}\n  clean/{client}/{project}/{year}/{month}/{stem__{sha8}}.md\n  index/\n    manifests/{client}/{project}/{year}/{month}/{stem__{sha8}}.json\n    sessions.jsonl\n    search.sqlite\n```\n\n**Manifest Keys:**\n```python\n{\n    \"client\": str,\n    \"project\": str,\n    \"session_id\": str,\n    \"title\": str,\n    \"started_at\": str,\n    \"imported_at\": str,\n    \"cwd\": str,\n    \"branch\": str,\n    \"raw_source_path\": str,\n    \"raw_sha256\": str,\n    \"raw_upload_permission\": str,\n    \"repo_raw_path\": str,  # relative to repo_root\n    \"repo_clean_path\": str,\n    \"repo_manifest_path\": str,\n    \"global_raw_path\": str,\n    \"global_clean_path\": str,\n    \"global_manifest_path\": str,\n    \"message_count\": int,\n    \"tool_event_count\": int,\n    \"cleaning_mode\": \"deterministic-code\",\n    \"repo_mirror_enabled\": bool,\n}\n```\n\n**Edge Cases:**\n- Year/month partitioning from `started_at` timestamp\n- Artifact stem: `{session_label}__{raw_sha256[:8]}`\n- Preserves `imported_at` if signature (excluding `imported_at`) unchanged\n- Only appends to event log if manifest differs from existing\n\n---\n\n### 6. **_transcript_utils.py** (128 lines)\n**Purpose:** Backward-compatible re-export hub for all public APIs.\n\n**Exported from _transcript_common:**\n- Constants: `CLIENTS`, `TEXT_BLOCK_TYPES`, `SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES`, `TOOL_USE_TYPES`, `TOOL_RESULT_TYPES`\n- Functions: `deduplicate_*`, `slugify`, `sha256_file`, `normalize_timestamp`, `earliest_timestamp`, `parse_iso_timestamp`, `timestamp_partition`, `join_paragraphs`, `summarize_value`, `truncate`, `yaml_escape`, `ensure_*`, `get_nested`, `load_*`, `extract_*`, `find_first_mapping_with_keys`\n\n**Exported from _transcript_parse:**\n- Classes: `TranscriptMessage`, `TranscriptToolEvent`, `ParsedTranscript`\n- Functions: `infer_client`, `parse_transcript`, `parse_codex_transcript`, `parse_claude_transcript`, `parse_gemini_transcript`\n\n**Exported from _transcript_discover:**\n- Functions: `default_global_transcript_root`, `default_client_roots`, `discover_source_files`, `infer_project_slug`, `transcript_matches_repo`, `project_slugs_match`, `safe_file_mtime`\n\n**Exported from _transcript_import:**\n- Functions: `import_transcript`, `transcript_has_content`, `write_clean_markdown`, `preserve_existing_import_timestamp`, `manifest_signature`, `manifest_changed`, `write_json`, `append_jsonl`, `copy_file`\n\n**Exported from _transcript_index:**\n- Functions: `upsert_search_index`\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependencies (Directed Graph)\n\n```\n_transcript_common.py (no internal deps)\n  ↓\n_transcript_parse.py (imports _transcript_common)\n  ↓\n_transcript_index.py (no transcript deps, uses sqlite3)\n_transcript_discover.py (imports _transcript_common, _transcript_parse)\n_transcript_import.py (imports _transcript_common, _transcript_parse, _transcript_index)\n  ↓\n_transcript_utils.py (imports all above, re-exports)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Key Implementation Details for TypeScript Port\n\n1. **Timestamp Handling:** All timestamps normalized to ISO 8601 with UTC `Z` suffix. `fromisoformat()` compatibility requires converting `Z` → `+00:00`.\n2. **Deduplication:** Uses 16-char SHA256 hex prefix as content signature; keyed on `(timestamp, role, hash)` for messages and `(timestamp, hash)` for tool events.\n3. **Path Partitioning:** Year/month derived from `started_at` using format `%Y` and `%m`.\n4. **SQLite Upsert:** Primary key is 4-tuple; all fields updated on conflict.\n5. **Gemini Format:** Recursive tree traversal; metadata and messages scattered throughout structure.\n6. **Claude Format:** Array of blocks with role and content; tool use/result are block types.\n7. **Codex Format:** JSONL with explicit record types (`session_meta`, `response_item`, `event_msg`) and payload sub-structure.\n\nAll files are available at:\n- `/D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/scripts/_transcript_common.py`\n- `/D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/scripts/_transcript_parse.py`\n- `/D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/scripts/_transcript_index.py`\n- `/D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/scripts/_transcript_discover.py`\n- `/D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/scripts/_transcript_import.py`\n- `/D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/scripts/_transcript_utils.py`",

    "text": "Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary document based on all the information I've gathered.\n\n## TypeScript Project Structure Exploration — Comprehensive Report\n\n### 1. PROJECT OVERVIEW\n\n**memorytree-workflow** is a Phase 0 scaffold TypeScript project that will eventually be a complete rewrite of the Python MemoryTree Skill. It provides transcript import, dedup, indexing, and session continuity for AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI).\n\n**Current Status:** Phase 0 complete (skeleton scaffolded)\n**Target Node.js:** 20+ (LTS) \n**Type Safety:** TypeScript 5.7 with strict mode enabled\n\n---\n\n### 2. PACKAGE.JSON CONFIGURATION\n\n**File:** `D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\package.json`\n\n```json\n{\n  \"name\": \"memorytree-workflow\",\n  \"version\": \"0.1.0\",\n  \"type\": \"module\",              // ESM only\n  \"engines\": { \"node\": \">=20\" }\n  \"scripts\": {\n    \"build\": \"tsup\",\n    \"dev\": \"tsx src/cli/main.ts\",\n    \"test\": \"vitest run\",\n    \"lint\": \"eslint src tests --ext .ts\",\n    \"typecheck\": \"tsc --noEmit\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Key Dependencies:**\n- `commander@^13.1.0` — CLI framework\n- `sql.js@^1.12.0` — SQLite via WASM (zero native dependencies)\n- `smol-toml@^1.3.1` — TOML parsing\n- `@iarna/toml@^2.2.5` — TOML serialization\n\n**Dev Dependencies:**\n- `typescript@^5.7.3` — strict mode compiler\n- `tsx@^4.19.3` — TypeScript executor (dev only)\n- `tsup@^8.4.0` — bundler for production\n- `vitest@^3.1.1` — test runner (globals enabled)\n- `@types/node@^22.13.10` — Node.js type definitions\n- `eslint@^8.57.1` + `@typescript-eslint/*@^7.18.0` — linting\n- `memfs@^4.17.0` — in-memory filesystem for testing\n\n**Bin Entries:**\n```json\n\"bin\": {\n  \"memorytree\": \"./dist/cli.js\",\n  \"memorytree-daemon\": \"./dist/cli.js\"\n}\n```\nBoth point to the same compiled CLI entry point.\n\n---\n\n### 3. TYPESCRIPT CONFIGURATION (tsconfig.json)\n\n**File:** `D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\tsconfig.json`\n\n**Key Settings:**\n- **Target:** `ES2022` (modern JavaScript features)\n- **Module:** `NodeNext` (requires `.js` extensions in imports)\n- **Output:** `outDir: \"dist\"`, `rootDir: \"src\"`\n- **Strict Mode:** Fully enabled\n  - `strict: true`\n  - `exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true` — optional properties cannot be `undefined`\n  - `noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true` — array access returns `T | undefined`\n- **Source Maps:** Enabled for production debugging\n- **Declaration Files:** Disabled (CLI tool, not a library)\n\n**Impact:** All paths in imports must include `.js` extension:\n```typescript\nimport { toPosixPath } from './utils/path.js'  // ✓ correct\nimport { toPosixPath } from './utils/path'      // ✗ fails\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4. BUILD & TEST CONFIGURATION\n\n**tsup.config.ts** — Bundler Configuration:\n```typescript\nexport default defineConfig({\n  entry: { cli: 'src/cli/main.ts' },\n  format: ['esm'],\n  target: 'node20',\n  sourcemap: true,\n  clean: true,\n  dts: false,  // No type definitions for CLI tool\n  banner: { js: '#!/usr/bin/env node' }\n})\n```\n- Single entry point: `src/cli/main.ts` → `dist/cli.js`\n- Shebang automatically added for Unix execution\n- OS scheduler registers: `node dist/cli.js` (not `tsx src/...`)\n\n**vitest.config.ts** — Test Configuration:\n```typescript\nexport default defineConfig({\n  test: {\n    include: ['tests/**/*.test.ts'],\n    globals: true,  // describe/it/expect available without imports\n  },\n})\n```\n\n**.eslintrc.cjs** — Linting Rules:\n- Parser: `@typescript-eslint/parser`\n- Extends: `eslint:recommended` + `plugin:@typescript-eslint/recommended`\n- Rule: `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` ignores parameters starting with `_`\n\n---\n\n### 5. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE & SOURCE FILES\n\n#### **src/types/** — Type Definitions\n\n**transcript.ts** (49 lines):\nDefines all shared interfaces for transcript processing:\n\n```typescript\ntype Client = 'codex' | 'claude' | 'gemini'\n\ninterface TranscriptMessage {\n  role: string\n  text: string\n  timestamp: string | null\n}\n\ninterface TranscriptToolEvent {\n  summary: string\n  timestamp: string | null\n}\n\ninterface ParsedTranscript {\n  client: Client\n  session_id: string\n  title: string\n  started_at: string\n  cwd: string\n  branch: string\n  messages: TranscriptMessage[]\n  tool_events: TranscriptToolEvent[]\n  source_path: string  // posix format absolute path\n}\n\ninterface ManifestEntry {\n  client: string\n  project: string\n  session_id: string\n  raw_sha256: string\n  title: string\n  started_at: string\n  imported_at: string\n  cwd: string\n  branch: string\n  raw_source_path: string\n  raw_upload_permission: string\n  global_raw_path: string\n  global_clean_path: string\n  repo_raw_path: string\n  repo_clean_path: string\n  repo_manifest_path: string\n  message_count: number\n  tool_event_count: number\n}\n```\n\n**Note:** These interfaces are Phase 1 contracts. Currently they are skeleton stubs.\n\n#### **src/utils/** — Utility Functions\n\n**path.ts** (10 lines, IMPLEMENTED):\n```typescript\nexport function toPosixPath(p: string): string {\n  return normalize(p).replace(/\\\\/g, '/')\n}\n```\n- Converts all paths to forward slashes for persistent storage\n- All writes to JSON/SQLite/Markdown must use posix paths\n- Handles empty strings → `'.'`\n- Normalizes redundant separators and dot segments\n\n**exec.ts** — Subprocess Wrapper (placeholder)\nTo be implemented in Phase 1/2. Will wrap `child_process.execSync` with error handling.\n\n#### **src/transcript/** — Transcript Processing (Phase 1)\n\nAll currently skeleton stubs. Implementation order per TODO-TS-MIGRATION.md:\n\n1. **common.ts** — Constants, utilities, text extraction, dedup (22 functions)\n   - Constants: `CLIENTS`, `TEXT_BLOCK_TYPES`, `SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES`, `TOOL_USE_TYPES`, `TOOL_RESULT_TYPES`\n   - Text extraction: `extractTextBlocks()`, `extractSimpleText()`, `extractGeminiText()`, `extractGeminiParts()`\n   - Dedup: `deduplicateMessages()`, `deduplicateToolEvents()` (signature-based on timestamp + role + sha256_prefix)\n   - Utilities: `slugify()`, `sha256File()`, `contentHash()`, `normalizeTimestamp()`, `joinParagraphs()`, etc.\n\n2. **parse.ts** — Three client parsers + client inference\n   - `parseCodexTranscript(filePath)` — JSONL format\n   - `parseClaudeTranscript(filePath)` — JSONL format\n   - `parseGeminiTranscript(filePath)` — JSON format\n   - `inferClient(client, sourcePath)` — auto-detection from path\n   - `parseTranscript(client, sourcePath)` — dispatcher\n\n3. **import.ts** — Import, clean, manifest (9 functions)\n   - `importTranscript()` — raw copy + clean markdown + manifest write + SQLite upsert\n   - `writeCleanMarkdown()` — YAML frontmatter format\n   - Idempotent functions: `manifestSignature()`, `manifestChanged()`, `preserveExistingImportTimestamp()`\n   - File operations: `writeJson()`, `appendJsonl()`, `copyFile()`\n\n4. **db.ts** — SQLite UPSERT index via sql.js\n   - `upsertSearchIndex(dbPath, manifest)` — 18-field UPSERT table\n   - Primary key: `(client, project, session_id, raw_sha256)`\n   - WAL mode enabled for concurrent writes\n   - Async API (sql.js is WASM-based)\n\n5. **discover.ts** — File discovery, project matching (7 functions)\n   - `discoverSourceFiles(clients?)` — glob client directories by mtime\n   - `defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot()` — `~/.memorytree/transcripts`\n   - `defaultClientRoots()` — paths for Codex, Claude, Gemini\n   - `inferProjectSlug(parsed)` — extract project name from CWD/path\n   - `transcriptMatchesRepo()` — check if transcript belongs to repo\n   - `projectSlugsMatch()` — compare project names\n\n6. **index.ts** — Barrel re-export (public API only)\n   Currently placeholder; will export public functions from other modules.\n\n#### **src/heartbeat/** — Heartbeat & Daemon (Phase 2)\n\nAll currently skeleton stubs:\n\n1. **config.ts** — Config type + TOML load/save/project registration\n   - Interface: `ProjectEntry`, `Config` (heartbeat_interval, watch_dirs, projects, auto_push, log_level)\n   - Functions: `memorytreeRoot()`, `configPath()`, `loadConfig()`, `saveConfig()`, `intervalToSeconds()`, `registerProject()`\n\n2. **lock.ts** — PID-based lock + stale detection\n   - `acquireLock()` — atomic `O_CREAT | O_EXCL` write\n   - `releaseLock()` — delete lock file\n   - `isProcessAlive(pid)` — platform-specific checks (kill signal on Unix, tasklist on Windows)\n   - Stale detection: auto-recover if process PID no longer exists\n\n3. **alert.ts** — alerts.json management + threshold\n   - `writeAlert()` — append + dedup (max 100)\n   - `writeAlertWithThreshold()` — write only after N consecutive failures\n   - `resetFailureCount()` — reset failure state\n   - `formatAlertsForDisplay()` — format for CLI output\n\n4. **log.ts** — Logging setup with file rotation + stderr\n   - `setupLogging(logLevel)` — dual output (stderr + file)\n   - File path: `~/.memorytree/logs/heartbeat-YYYY-MM-DD.log`\n   - Daily rotation (new file per day)\n   - Format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS [LEVEL] message`\n\n5. **heartbeat.ts** — Main heartbeat logic + sensitive pattern scanning\n   - `main()` — acquire lock → runHeartbeat → release lock\n   - `runHeartbeat(config)` — iterate projects, run `processProject()` for each\n   - `processProject()` — discover → parse → filter → scanSensitive → import → git commit + push\n   - `scanSensitive()` — 5 regex patterns for API keys, passwords, tokens\n   - `gitCommitAndPush()` — stage Memory/ → commit → optional push\n   - `git()` — subprocess wrapper for git operations\n\n#### **src/project/** — Project Utilities (Phase 3)\n\n1. **scaffold.ts** — Directory init + template rendering\n   - `resolveScaffoldPaths()` — compute all Memory/ paths and version numbers\n   - `createMemoryDirs()` — create 5 standard subdirectories\n   - `scaffoldContentFiles()` — render templates with substitution\n   - Template handling: support `en` and `zh-cn` locales\n   - Version detection: `findLatestVersion()`, `findLatestGoalFile()`, etc.\n\n2. **locale.ts** — Project language detection\n   - `detectLocale(projectDir)` — regex pattern matching + scoring\n   - Output: JSON with `requested`, `repo_locale`, `system_locale`, `effective_locale`\n\n3. **upgrade.ts** — Upgrade detection + execution\n   - `detectState()` — 'not-installed' | 'partial' | 'installed'\n   - `checkUpgrade()` — detect missing directories/files\n   - AGENTS.md completeness: 9 signal detection functions\n\n#### **src/recall/** — Cross-Session Context Recovery (Phase 2.7)\n\n**recall.ts**:\n- `syncCurrentProject()` — incremental sync for current repo\n- `findLatestSession()` — SQLite query + CWD matching\n- `findLatestFromJsonl()` — fallback when SQLite unavailable\n- `cwdMatches()` — exact or subdirectory match\n- Output: JSON with session metadata + clean transcript content\n\n#### **src/cli/** — Command-Line Interface (Phase 2 & 4)\n\nAll currently skeleton stubs. Total of 8 command modules:\n\n1. **main.ts** (11 lines, IMPLEMENTED):\n   ```typescript\n   import { Command } from 'commander'\n   \n   const program = new Command()\n   program\n     .name('memorytree')\n     .description('MemoryTree — transcript import, dedup, indexing, and session continuity')\n     .version('0.1.0')\n   program.parse()\n   ```\n   - Commander-based CLI framework\n   - Subcommands to be added incrementally\n\n2. **cmd-init.ts** — `memorytree init` (Phase 4)\n   - Parameters: `--root`, `--project-name`, `--goal-summary`, `--locale`, `--date`, `--time`, `--skip-agents`, `--force`\n\n3. **cmd-import.ts** — `memorytree import` (Phase 4)\n   - Parameters: `--root`, `--client`, `--scope`, `--project-name`, `--global-root`, `--raw-upload-permission`, `--limit`, `--format`\n   - Output: structured JSON\n\n4. **cmd-discover.ts** — `memorytree discover` (Phase 4)\n   - Similar parameters to import; returns list without importing\n\n5. **cmd-locale.ts** — `memorytree locale` (Phase 4)\n   - Parameters: `--root`, `--format`\n\n6. **cmd-recall.ts** — `memorytree recall` (Phase 4)\n   - Parameters: `--root`, `--project-name`, `--global-root`, `--activation-time`, `--format`\n\n7. **cmd-daemon.ts** — `memorytree daemon install|uninstall|run-once|watch|status` (Phase 2)\n   - Platform-specific scheduler registration (cron/launchd/schtasks)\n   - Includes Python→TypeScript migration detection\n\n8. **cmd-upgrade.ts** — `memorytree upgrade` (Phase 4)\n   - Parameters: `--root`, `--locale`, `--dry-run`\n\n---\n\n### 6. TEST INFRASTRUCTURE\n\n**Location:** `D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\tests/`\n\n**Current Test Files:**\n\n1. **smoke.test.ts** (25 lines, IMPLEMENTED):\n   - Tests `toPosixPath()` function\n   - 5 test cases: Windows paths, POSIX paths, redundant separators, empty strings, dot segments\n   - Uses vitest globals (describe/it/expect)\n\n2. **transcript/common.test.ts** (7 lines, PLACEHOLDER):\n   - Skeleton with todo placeholder for Phase 1\n\n**Planned Test Structure:**\n```\ntests/\n  fixtures/              # Python golden file outputs\n  transcript/\n    common.test.ts       # Phase 1.2\n    parse-codex.test.ts  # Phase 1.3a\n    parse-claude.test.ts # Phase 1.3b\n    parse-gemini.test.ts # Phase 1.3c\n    import.test.ts       # Phase 1.4\n    db.test.ts          # Phase 1.5\n    discover.test.ts    # Phase 1.6\n  heartbeat/\n    config.test.ts      # Phase 2.1\n    lock.test.ts        # Phase 2.2\n    alert.test.ts       # Phase 2.3\n    log.test.ts         # Phase 2.4\n    heartbeat.test.ts   # Phase 2.5\n  recall/\n    recall.test.ts      # Phase 2.7\n  project/\n    scaffold.test.ts    # Phase 3.1\n    locale.test.ts      # Phase 3.2\n    upgrade.test.ts     # Phase 3.3\n  cli/\n    daemon.test.ts      # Phase 2.6\n  integration/\n    e2e.test.ts         # End-to-end scenarios\n```\n\n**Testing Strategy:**\n- Minimum 80% coverage (vitest + coverage tools)\n- Test-driven development: write tests first (RED) → implement (GREEN) → refactor (IMPROVE)\n- Golden file validation: TS outputs must match Python reference outputs byte-for-byte\n- Mock strategy:\n  - Filesystem: `memfs` or `vi.mock('node:fs')`\n  - Subprocess: `vi.mock('node:child_process')`\n  - Time: `vi.setSystemTime()`\n  - SQLite: sql.js in-memory database\n  - Environment: `vi.stubEnv()`\n\n---\n\n### 7. ASSETS & TEMPLATES\n\n**Location:** `D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\assets/`\n\n**Template Files** (for project initialization):\n```\nassets/\n  logo.svg\n  templates/\n    en/\n      agents.md      # AI behavior rules template\n      chat-log.md    # Session log template\n      goal.md        # Project goal template\n      todo.md        # Task list template\n    zh-cn/           # Simplified Chinese versions\n      agents.md\n      chat-log.md\n      goal.md\n      todo.md\n```\n\nThese are static assets that will be bundled with the compiled CLI.\n\n---\n\n### 10. DOCUMENTATION\n\n**Key Reference Files:**\n\n1. **TODO-TS-MIGRATION.md** (741 lines) — Comprehensive 5-phase migration plan\n   - Phase 0: Project scaffold (DONE)\n   - Phase 1: Core transcript modules (40% of work)\n   - Phase 2: Heartbeat + daemon + recall\n   - Phase 3: Scaffold, locale, upgrade utilities\n   - Phase 4: CLI unification + cleanup + distribution\n\n2. **README.md** (323 lines) — Project overview\n   - Installation, usage, architecture\n   - Features: memory management, transcript import, cross-project search, session continuity\n   - Git safety rules, sensitive info scanning, locale support\n\n3. **references/** — Detailed architecture specifications\n   - `project-detection.md` — Install state detection\n   - `memory-layout.md` — Folder and file naming rules\n   - `update-rules.md` — Versioning strategy\n   - `git-policy.md` — Git commit/push rules\n   - `heartbeat-scheduling.md` — Daemon architecture\n   - `global-configuration.md` — `~/.memorytree/` layout\n   - `transcript-archive.md` — Cleaning and search\n   - `locale-selection.md` — Language detection\n   - `response-language.md` — Reply language precedence\n   - `execution-environment.md` — Runtime detection\n   - `upgrade-path.md` — Safe adoption for partial installs\n\n---\n\n### 11. BUILD OUTPUT\n\n**Compiled CLI:** `D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\dist\\cli.js` (291 bytes)\n\n- Generated from `src/cli/main.ts` via tsup\n- Shebang present: `#!/usr/bin/env node`\n- Source map: `dist/cli.js.map` (445 bytes)\n- Currently minimal (just command registration; no subcommands implemented)\n\n---\n\n### 12. KEY ARCHITECTURAL DECISIONS (Phase 0 Lock)\n\n1. **ESM Only** — No CommonJS compatibility; all imports require `.js` extensions\n2. **sql.js over better-sqlite3** — Zero native dependencies; WASM-based\n3. **smol-toml + @iarna/toml** — Parse (read) + stringify (write) split\n4. **No Result Type** — Use try/catch (consistent with Python error handling)\n5. **Immutable Patterns** — All updates return new objects; no mutations\n6. **Posix Paths** — All files persist paths in forward-slash format\n7. **Async for db operations** — sql.js requires async/await\n8. **Source maps enabled** — Stack traces readable in production\n\n---\n\n### 13. SUMMARY TABLE\n\n| Aspect | Details |\n|--------|---------|\n| **Language** | TypeScript 5.7 (strict) |\n| **Runtime** | Node.js 20+ |\n| **Test Framework** | vitest with globals |\n| **Build Tool** | tsup (bundles to ESM) |\n| **CLI Framework** | commander |\n| **SQLite** | sql.js (WASM) |\n| **Linter** | ESLint + @typescript-eslint |\n| **Source Files** | 28 `.ts` files (mostly skeletons) |\n| **Implemented** | `toPosixPath()` only + `src/cli/main.ts` stub |\n| **Test Files** | 2 (smoke.test.ts + placeholder) |\n| **Coverage Goal** | 80%+ |\n| **CI Platforms** | ubuntu, macos, windows |\n| **Dist Output** | `dist/cli.js` with shebang + source map |\n| **Package Type** | ESM module |\n| **Bin Aliases** | `memorytree`, `memorytree-daemon` |\n\n---\n\nThis is a **Phase 0 skeleton** — all structural files are in place, build pipeline configured, one utility function implemented, CI configured for both Python (legacy) and TypeScript (new). Ready for Phase 1 core transcript module implementation.",

1→"""Shared constants, data-access helpers, and text-extraction utilities."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→import hashlib
     6→import json
     7→import re
     8→from datetime import datetime, timezone
     9→from pathlib import Path
    10→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
    11→
    12→if TYPE_CHECKING:
    13→    from _transcript_parse import TranscriptMessage, TranscriptToolEvent
    14→
    15→
    16→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→# Constants
    18→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    19→
    20→CLIENTS = {"codex", "claude", "gemini"}
    21→TEXT_BLOCK_TYPES = {"input_text", "output_text", "text"}
    22→SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES = {"thinking", "reasoning"}
    23→TOOL_USE_TYPES = {"tool_use"}
    24→TOOL_RESULT_TYPES = {"tool_result"}
    25→
    26→
    27→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    28→# Generic utilities
    29→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    30→
    31→
    32→def slugify(value: str, fallback: str = "session") -> str:
    33→    ascii_value = value.encode("ascii", "ignore").decode("ascii").lower()
    34→    slug = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9._-]+", "-", ascii_value).strip("-._")
    35→    return slug or fallback
    36→
    37→
    38→def sha256_file(path: Path) -> str:
    39→    digest = hashlib.sha256()
    40→    with path.open("rb") as handle:
    41→        for chunk in iter(lambda: handle.read(1024 * 1024), b""):
    42→            digest.update(chunk)
    43→    return digest.hexdigest()
    44→
    45→
    46→def normalize_timestamp(value: Any, fallback: str | None = None) -> str:
    47→    if isinstance(value, (int, float)):
    48→        return (
    49→            datetime.fromtimestamp(float(value), tz=timezone.utc)
    50→            .replace(microsecond=0)
    51→            .isoformat()
    52→            .replace("+00:00", "Z")
    53→        )
    54→    if isinstance(value, datetime):
    55→        normalized = value.astimezone(timezone.utc) if value.tzinfo else value
    56→        return normalized.replace(microsecond=0).isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z")
    57→    if isinstance(value, str):
    58→        text = value.strip()
    59→        if not text:
    60→            return fallback or normalize_timestamp(datetime.now(timezone.utc))
    61→        iso_candidate = text[:-1] + "+00:00" if text.endswith("Z") else text
    62→        try:
    63→            parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(iso_candidate)
    64→            if parsed.tzinfo is not None:
    65→                parsed = parsed.astimezone(timezone.utc)
    66→            return parsed.replace(microsecond=0).isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z")
    67→        except ValueError:
    68→            return text
    69→    return fallback or normalize_timestamp(datetime.now(timezone.utc))
    70→
    71→
    72→def earliest_timestamp(current: str, candidate: Any) -> str:
    73→    normalized = normalize_timestamp(candidate, current)
    74→    current_dt = parse_iso_timestamp(current)
    75→    normalized_dt = parse_iso_timestamp(normalized)
    76→    if current_dt is not None and normalized_dt is not None:
    77→        return normalized if normalized_dt < current_dt else current
    78→    if normalized_dt is not None:
    79→        return normalized
    80→    return current or normalized
    81→
    82→
    83→def parse_iso_timestamp(value: str) -> datetime | None:
    84→    if not value:
    85→        return None
    86→    candidate = value[:-1] + "+00:00" if value.endswith("Z") else value
    87→    try:
    88→        parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(candidate)
    89→    except ValueError:
    90→        return None
    91→    if parsed.tzinfo is None:
    92→        return parsed.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    93→    return parsed.astimezone(timezone.utc)
    94→
    95→
    96→def timestamp_partition(timestamp: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
    97→    try:
    98→        candidate = timestamp[:-1] + "+00:00" if timestamp.endswith("Z") else timestamp
    99→        parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(candidate)
   100→        return parsed.strftime("%Y"), parsed.strftime("%m")
   101→    except ValueError:
   102→        return "unknown", "unknown"
   103→
   104→
   105→def join_paragraphs(parts: Any) -> str:
   106→    cleaned = [str(part).strip() for part in parts if str(part).strip()]
   107→    return "\n\n".join(cleaned)
   108→
   109→
   110→def summarize_value(value: Any, limit: int = 180) -> str:
   111→    if value is None:
   112→        return "none"
   113→    if isinstance(value, str):
   114→        text = " ".join(value.split())
   115→        return truncate(text, limit)
   116→    try:
   117→        text = json.dumps(value, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True)
   118→    except TypeError:
   119→        text = str(value)
   120→    text = " ".join(text.split())
   121→    return truncate(text, limit)
   122→
   123→
   124→def truncate(text: str, limit: int) -> str:
   125→    return text if len(text) <= limit else text[: limit - 3] + "..."
   126→
   127→
   128→def yaml_escape(value: str) -> str:
   129→    escaped = value.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace('"', '\\"').replace("\n", "\\n")
   130→    return f'"{escaped}"'
   131→
   132→
   133→def ensure_dict(value: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
   134→    return value if isinstance(value, dict) else {}
   135→
   136→
   137→def ensure_list(value: Any) -> list[Any]:
   138→    return value if isinstance(value, list) else []
   139→
   140→
   141→def get_nested(payload: dict[str, Any], *keys: str) -> Any:
   142→    current: Any = payload
   143→    for key in keys:
   144→        if not isinstance(current, dict):
   145→            return None
   146→        current = current.get(key)
   147→    return current
   148→
   149→
   150→def load_jsonl(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   151→    records: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   152→    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as handle:
   153→        for line in handle:
   154→            line = line.strip()
   155→            if not line:
   156→                continue
   157→            try:
   158→                records.append(json.loads(line))
   159→            except json.JSONDecodeError:
   160→                continue
   161→    return records
   162→
   163→
   164→def load_json(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
   165→    if not path.exists():
   166→        return None
   167→    try:
   168→        return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   169→    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
   170→        return None
   171→
   172→
   173→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   174→# Text extraction helpers
   175→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   176→
   177→
   178→def extract_text_blocks(blocks: Any) -> str:
   179→    parts: list[str] = []
   180→    for block in ensure_list(blocks):
   181→        if isinstance(block, str):
   182→            text = block.strip()
   183→            if text:
   184→                parts.append(text)
   185→            continue
   186→        if not isinstance(block, dict):
   187→            continue
   188→        block_type = str(block.get("type") or "").lower()
   189→        if block_type in SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES | TOOL_USE_TYPES | TOOL_RESULT_TYPES:
   190→            continue
   191→        if block_type in TEXT_BLOCK_TYPES:
   192→            text = str(block.get("text") or "").strip()
   193→            if text:
   194→                parts.append(text)
   195→            continue
   196→        fallback_text = extract_simple_text(block)
   197→        if fallback_text:
   198→            parts.append(fallback_text)
   199→    return join_paragraphs(parts)
   200→
   201→
   202→def extract_simple_text(value: Any) -> str:
   203→    if isinstance(value, str):
   204→        return value.strip()
   205→    if isinstance(value, list):
   206→        return join_paragraphs(extract_simple_text(item) for item in value)
   207→    if isinstance(value, dict):
   208→        for key in ("text", "content", "value"):
   209→            if key in value and isinstance(value[key], str):
   210→                return value[key].strip()
   211→        return ""
   212→    return ""
   213→
   214→
   215→def extract_gemini_text(node: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
   216→    parts: list[str] = []
   217→    for candidate in (node.get("parts"), node.get("content"), node.get("text"), node.get("message")):
   218→        parts.extend(extract_gemini_parts(candidate))
   219→    return join_paragraphs(parts)
   220→
   221→
   222→def extract_gemini_parts(value: Any) -> list[str]:
   223→    if isinstance(value, str):
   224→        text = value.strip()
   225→        return [text] if text else []
   226→    if isinstance(value, list):
   227→        parts: list[str] = []
   228→        for item in value:
   229→            parts.extend(extract_gemini_parts(item))
   230→        return parts
   231→    if isinstance(value, dict):
   232→        block_type = str(value.get("type") or "").lower()
   233→        if block_type in SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES | TOOL_USE_TYPES | TOOL_RESULT_TYPES:
   234→            return []
   235→        if "text" in value and isinstance(value["text"], str):
   236→            text = value["text"].strip()
   237→            return [text] if text else []
   238→        parts: list[str] = []
   239→        for key in ("parts", "content"):
   240→            if key in value:
   241→                parts.extend(extract_gemini_parts(value[key]))
   242→        return parts
   243→    return []
   244→
   245→
   246→def find_first_mapping_with_keys(value: Any, keys: set[str]) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
   247→    if isinstance(value, dict):
   248→        if keys & set(value.keys()):
   249→            return value
   250→        for candidate in value.values():
   251→            result = find_first_mapping_with_keys(candidate, keys)
   252→            if result is not None:
   253→                return result
   254→        return None
   255→    if isinstance(value, list):
   256→        for candidate in value:
   257→            result = find_first_mapping_with_keys(candidate, keys)
   258→            if result is not None:
   259→                return result
   260→    return None
   261→
   262→
   263→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   264→# Deduplication helpers
   265→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   266→
   267→
   268→def _content_hash(text: str) -> str:
   269→    return hashlib.sha256(text.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]
   270→
   271→
   272→def deduplicate_messages(messages: list[TranscriptMessage]) -> list[TranscriptMessage]:
   273→    """Deduplicate based on (timestamp, role, content_sha256_prefix). Keep first."""
   274→    seen: set[tuple[str | None, str, str]] = set()
   275→    result: list[TranscriptMessage] = []
   276→    for msg in messages:
   277→        sig = (msg.timestamp, msg.role, _content_hash(msg.text))
   278→        if sig not in seen:
   279→            seen.add(sig)
   280→            result.append(msg)
   281→    return result
   282→
   283→
   284→def deduplicate_tool_events(events: list[TranscriptToolEvent]) -> list[TranscriptToolEvent]:
   285→    """Deduplicate based on (timestamp, summary_sha256_prefix). Keep first."""
   286→    seen: set[tuple[str | None, str]] = set()
   287→    result: list[TranscriptToolEvent] = []
   288→    for evt in events:
   289→        sig = (evt.timestamp, _content_hash(evt.summary))
   290→        if sig not in seen:
   291→            seen.add(sig)
   292→            result.append(evt)
   293→    return result
   294→
Tool Result #16 Read toolu_013kWCiUsLK7RXHybi5zr6FH 09:22:52
Read output=1→"""Transcript parsing — dataclasses, client inference, and format-specific parsers.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→import json 6→from dataclasses import datac...
Show output payload 
1→"""Transcript parsing — dataclasses, client inference, and format-specific parsers."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→import json
     6→from dataclasses import dataclass
     7→from pathlib import Path
     8→from typing import Any
     9→
    10→from _transcript_common import *  # noqa: F401,F403
    11→from _transcript_common import (
    12→    SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES,
    13→    TEXT_BLOCK_TYPES,
    14→    TOOL_RESULT_TYPES,
    15→    TOOL_USE_TYPES,
    16→    deduplicate_messages,
    17→    deduplicate_tool_events,
    18→    earliest_timestamp,
    19→    ensure_dict,
    20→    ensure_list,
    21→    extract_gemini_text,
    22→    extract_text_blocks,
    23→    find_first_mapping_with_keys,
    24→    get_nested,
    25→    join_paragraphs,
    26→    load_jsonl,
    27→    normalize_timestamp,
    28→    summarize_value,
    29→)
    30→
    31→
    32→@dataclass
    33→class TranscriptMessage:
    34→    role: str
    35→    text: str
    36→    timestamp: str | None = None
    37→
    38→
    39→@dataclass
    40→class TranscriptToolEvent:
    41→    summary: str
    42→    timestamp: str | None = None
    43→
    44→
    45→@dataclass
    46→class ParsedTranscript:
    47→    client: str
    48→    session_id: str
    49→    title: str
    50→    started_at: str
    51→    cwd: str
    52→    branch: str
    53→    messages: list[TranscriptMessage]
    54→    tool_events: list[TranscriptToolEvent]
    55→    source_path: Path
    56→
    57→
    58→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    59→# Client inference + dispatch
    60→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    61→
    62→
    63→def infer_client(client: str, source: Path) -> str:
    64→    if client != "auto":
    65→        return client
    66→    normalized = source.as_posix().lower()
    67→    if "/.codex/" in normalized or "rollout-" in source.name.lower():
    68→        return "codex"
    69→    if "/.claude/" in normalized or "/projects/" in normalized:
    70→        return "claude"
    71→    if "/.gemini/" in normalized or "checkpoint" in normalized:
    72→        return "gemini"
    73→    raise SystemExit(f"could not infer transcript client from source path: {source}")
    74→
    75→
    76→def parse_transcript(client: str, source: Path) -> ParsedTranscript:
    77→    if client == "codex":
    78→        return parse_codex_transcript(source)
    79→    if client == "claude":
    80→        return parse_claude_transcript(source)
    81→    if client == "gemini":
    82→        return parse_gemini_transcript(source)
    83→    raise SystemExit(f"unsupported transcript client: {client}")
    84→
    85→
    86→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    87→# Codex parser
    88→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    89→
    90→
    91→def parse_codex_transcript(path: Path) -> ParsedTranscript:
    92→    records = load_jsonl(path)
    93→    session_id = path.stem
    94→    title = path.stem
    95→    started_at = normalize_timestamp(path.stat().st_mtime)
    96→    cwd = ""
    97→    branch = ""
    98→    messages: list[TranscriptMessage] = []
    99→    tool_events: list[TranscriptToolEvent] = []
   100→
   101→    for record in records:
   102→        record_type = str(record.get("type") or "")
   103→        timestamp = normalize_timestamp(record.get("timestamp"), started_at)
   104→        if record_type == "session_meta":
   105→            payload = ensure_dict(record.get("payload"))
   106→            session_id = str(payload.get("id") or session_id)
   107→            title = str(payload.get("thread_name") or payload.get("title") or title)
   108→            started_at = earliest_timestamp(started_at, payload.get("timestamp"))
   109→            cwd = str(payload.get("cwd") or cwd)
   110→            branch = str(get_nested(payload, "git", "branch") or branch)
   111→            continue
   112→
   113→        if record_type not in {"response_item", "event_msg"}:
   114→            continue
   115→
   116→        payload = ensure_dict(record.get("payload"))
   117→        payload_type = str(payload.get("type") or "")
   118→        if payload_type == "message":
   119→            role = str(payload.get("role") or "").lower()
   120→            if role in {"user", "assistant"}:
   121→                text = extract_text_blocks(payload.get("content"))
   122→                if text:
   123→                    messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
   124→            continue
   125→
   126→        if payload_type in {"user_message", "agent_message"}:
   127→            role = "user" if payload_type == "user_message" else "assistant"
   128→            text = str(payload.get("message") or "").strip()
   129→            if text:
   130→                messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
   131→            continue
   132→
   133→        if payload_type == "function_call":
   134→            name = str(payload.get("name") or "function_call")
   135→            arguments = payload.get("arguments") or payload.get("input")
   136→            tool_events.append(
   137→                TranscriptToolEvent(
   138→                    summary=f"{name} input={summarize_value(arguments)}",
   139→                    timestamp=timestamp,
   140→                )
   141→            )
   142→            continue
   143→
   144→        if payload_type == "custom_tool_call":
   145→            name = str(payload.get("name") or payload.get("call_id") or "custom_tool_call")
   146→            tool_events.append(
   147→                TranscriptToolEvent(
   148→                    summary=f"{name} input={summarize_value(payload.get('input'))}",
   149→                    timestamp=timestamp,
   150→                )
   151→            )
   152→            continue
   153→
   154→        if payload_type == "function_call_output":
   155→            name = str(payload.get("name") or payload.get("call_id") or "function_call_output")
   156→            output = payload.get("output") or payload.get("content")
   157→            tool_events.append(
   158→                TranscriptToolEvent(
   159→                    summary=f"{name} output={summarize_value(output)}",
   160→                    timestamp=timestamp,
   161→                )
   162→            )
   163→            continue
   164→
   165→        if payload_type == "custom_tool_call_output":
   166→            name = str(payload.get("name") or payload.get("call_id") or "custom_tool_call_output")
   167→            output = payload.get("output") or payload.get("content")
   168→            tool_events.append(
   169→                TranscriptToolEvent(
   170→                    summary=f"{name} output={summarize_value(output)}",
   171→                    timestamp=timestamp,
   172→                )
   173→            )
   174→
   175→    messages = deduplicate_messages(messages)
   176→    tool_events = deduplicate_tool_events(tool_events)
   177→
   178→    return ParsedTranscript(
   179→        client="codex",
   180→        session_id=session_id,
   181→        title=title,
   182→        started_at=started_at,
   183→        cwd=cwd,
   184→        branch=branch,
   185→        messages=messages,
   186→        tool_events=tool_events,
   187→        source_path=path,
   188→    )
   189→
   190→
   191→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   192→# Claude parser
   193→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   194→
   195→
   196→def parse_claude_transcript(path: Path) -> ParsedTranscript:
   197→    records = load_jsonl(path)
   198→    session_id = path.stem
   199→    title = path.stem
   200→    started_at = normalize_timestamp(path.stat().st_mtime)
   201→    cwd = ""
   202→    branch = ""
   203→    messages: list[TranscriptMessage] = []
   204→    tool_events: list[TranscriptToolEvent] = []
   205→
   206→    for record in records:
   207→        record_type = str(record.get("type") or "")
   208→        timestamp = normalize_timestamp(record.get("timestamp"), started_at)
   209→        session_id = str(record.get("sessionId") or session_id)
   210→        started_at = earliest_timestamp(started_at, record.get("timestamp"))
   211→        cwd = str(record.get("cwd") or cwd)
   212→        branch = str(record.get("gitBranch") or branch)
   213→
   214→        if record_type not in {"user", "assistant"}:
   215→            continue
   216→
   217→        message = ensure_dict(record.get("message"))
   218→        role = str(message.get("role") or record_type).lower()
   219→        text_parts: list[str] = []
   220→        content = message.get("content")
   221→        if isinstance(content, str):
   222→            text = content.strip()
   223→            if role in {"user", "assistant"} and text:
   224→                messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
   225→            continue
   226→
   227→        for block in ensure_list(content):
   228→            if isinstance(block, str):
   229→                if block.strip():
   230→                    text_parts.append(block.strip())
   231→                continue
   232→            if not isinstance(block, dict):
   233→                continue
   234→            block_type = str(block.get("type") or "").lower()
   235→            if block_type in TEXT_BLOCK_TYPES:
   236→                text = str(block.get("text") or "").strip()
   237→                if text:
   238→                    text_parts.append(text)
   239→                continue
   240→            if block_type in SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES:
   241→                continue
   242→            if block_type in TOOL_USE_TYPES:
   243→                name = str(block.get("name") or "tool_use")
   244→                tool_events.append(
   245→                    TranscriptToolEvent(
   246→                        summary=f"{name} input={summarize_value(block.get('input'))}",
   247→                        timestamp=timestamp,
   248→                    )
   249→                )
   250→                continue
   251→            if block_type in TOOL_RESULT_TYPES:
   252→                tool_use_id = str(block.get("tool_use_id") or "tool_result")
   253→                tool_events.append(
   254→                    TranscriptToolEvent(
   255→                        summary=f"{tool_use_id} output={summarize_value(block.get('content'))}",
   256→                        timestamp=timestamp,
   257→                    )
   258→                )
   259→
   260→        text = join_paragraphs(text_parts)
   261→        if role in {"user", "assistant"} and text:
   262→            messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
   263→
   264→    messages = deduplicate_messages(messages)
   265→    tool_events = deduplicate_tool_events(tool_events)
   266→
   267→    return ParsedTranscript(
   268→        client="claude",
   269→        session_id=session_id,
   270→        title=title,
   271→        started_at=started_at,
   272→        cwd=cwd,
   273→        branch=branch,
   274→        messages=messages,
   275→        tool_events=tool_events,
   276→        source_path=path,
   277→    )
   278→
   279→
   280→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   281→# Gemini parser
   282→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   283→
   284→
   285→def parse_gemini_transcript(path: Path) -> ParsedTranscript:
   286→    session_id = path.stem
   287→    title = path.stem
   288→    started_at = normalize_timestamp(path.stat().st_mtime)
   289→    cwd = ""
   290→    branch = ""
   291→    messages: list[TranscriptMessage] = []
   292→    tool_events: list[TranscriptToolEvent] = []
   293→
   294→    if path.suffix.lower() == ".jsonl":
   295→        payload: Any = load_jsonl(path)
   296→    else:
   297→        payload = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   298→
   299→    first_meta = find_first_mapping_with_keys(payload, {"sessionId", "chatId", "cwd", "branch", "timestamp"})
   300→    if first_meta:
   301→        session_id = str(first_meta.get("sessionId") or first_meta.get("chatId") or first_meta.get("id") or session_id)
   302→        started_at = earliest_timestamp(started_at, first_meta.get("timestamp"))
   303→        cwd = str(first_meta.get("cwd") or first_meta.get("projectRoot") or cwd)
   304→        branch = str(first_meta.get("branch") or branch)
   305→
   306→    def visit(node: Any) -> None:
   307→        nonlocal session_id, started_at, cwd, branch
   308→        if isinstance(node, list):
   309→            for item in node:
   310→                visit(item)
   311→            return
   312→        if not isinstance(node, dict):
   313→            return
   314→
   315→        session_id = str(node.get("sessionId") or node.get("chatId") or node.get("id") or session_id)
   316→        started_at = earliest_timestamp(started_at, node.get("timestamp"))
   317→        cwd = str(node.get("cwd") or node.get("projectRoot") or cwd)
   318→        branch = str(node.get("branch") or branch)
   319→
   320→        role = str(node.get("role") or node.get("author") or node.get("sender") or "").lower()
   321→        timestamp = normalize_timestamp(node.get("timestamp"), started_at)
   322→        if role in {"user", "assistant", "model"}:
   323→            normalized_role = "assistant" if role == "model" else role
   324→            text = extract_gemini_text(node)
   325→            if text:
   326→                messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=normalized_role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
   327→
   328→        tool_name = (
   329→            get_nested(node, "toolUse", "name")
   330→            or node.get("toolName")
   331→            or node.get("functionName")
   332→            or node.get("tool")
   333→        )
   334→        if tool_name:
   335→            tool_events.append(
   336→                TranscriptToolEvent(
   337→                    summary=f"{tool_name} input={summarize_value(node.get('args') or node.get('input'))}",
   338→                    timestamp=timestamp,
   339→                )
   340→            )
   341→
   342→        for value in node.values():
   343→            visit(value)
   344→
   345→    visit(payload)
   346→
   347→    messages = deduplicate_messages(messages)
   348→    tool_events = deduplicate_tool_events(tool_events)
   349→
   350→    return ParsedTranscript(
   351→        client="gemini",
   352→        session_id=session_id,
   353→        title=title,
   354→        started_at=started_at,
   355→        cwd=cwd,
   356→        branch=branch,
   357→        messages=messages,
   358→        tool_events=tool_events,
   359→        source_path=path,
   360→    )
   361→

Read output=1→"""Transcript search index — SQLite upsert for the global transcript catalog.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→import sqlite3 6→from pathlib import Path 7→from ...

1→"""Transcript search index — SQLite upsert for the global transcript catalog."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→import sqlite3
     6→from pathlib import Path
     7→from typing import Any
     8→
     9→
    10→def upsert_search_index(db_path: Path, manifest: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    11→    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
    12→    try:
    13→        conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
    14→        conn.execute(
    15→            """
    16→            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transcripts (
    17→                client TEXT NOT NULL,
    18→                project TEXT NOT NULL,
    19→                session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    20→                raw_sha256 TEXT NOT NULL,
    21→                title TEXT NOT NULL,
    22→                started_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    23→                imported_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    24→                cwd TEXT NOT NULL,
    25→                branch TEXT NOT NULL,
    26→                raw_source_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    27→                raw_upload_permission TEXT NOT NULL,
    28→                global_raw_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    29→                global_clean_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    30→                repo_raw_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    31→                repo_clean_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    32→                repo_manifest_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    33→                message_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
    34→                tool_event_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
    35→                PRIMARY KEY (client, project, session_id, raw_sha256)
    36→            )
    37→            """
    38→        )
    39→        conn.execute(
    40→            """
    41→            INSERT INTO transcripts (
    42→                client,
    43→                project,
    44→                session_id,
    45→                raw_sha256,
    46→                title,
    47→                started_at,
    48→                imported_at,
    49→                cwd,
    50→                branch,
    51→                raw_source_path,
    52→                raw_upload_permission,
    53→                global_raw_path,
    54→                global_clean_path,
    55→                repo_raw_path,
    56→                repo_clean_path,
    57→                repo_manifest_path,
    58→                message_count,
    59→                tool_event_count
    60→            ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
    61→            ON CONFLICT(client, project, session_id, raw_sha256) DO UPDATE SET
    62→                title = excluded.title,
    63→                started_at = excluded.started_at,
    64→                imported_at = excluded.imported_at,
    65→                cwd = excluded.cwd,
    66→                branch = excluded.branch,
    67→                raw_source_path = excluded.raw_source_path,
    68→                raw_upload_permission = excluded.raw_upload_permission,
    69→                global_raw_path = excluded.global_raw_path,
    70→                global_clean_path = excluded.global_clean_path,
    71→                repo_raw_path = excluded.repo_raw_path,
    72→                repo_clean_path = excluded.repo_clean_path,
    73→                repo_manifest_path = excluded.repo_manifest_path,
    74→                message_count = excluded.message_count,
    75→                tool_event_count = excluded.tool_event_count
    76→            """,
    77→            (
    78→                manifest["client"],
    79→                manifest["project"],
    80→                manifest["session_id"],
    81→                manifest["raw_sha256"],
    82→                manifest["title"],
    83→                manifest["started_at"],
    84→                manifest["imported_at"],
    85→                manifest["cwd"],
    86→                manifest["branch"],
    87→                manifest["raw_source_path"],
    88→                manifest["raw_upload_permission"],
    89→                manifest["global_raw_path"],
    90→                manifest["global_clean_path"],
    91→                manifest["repo_raw_path"],
    92→                manifest["repo_clean_path"],
    93→                manifest["repo_manifest_path"],
    94→                manifest["message_count"],
    95→                manifest["tool_event_count"],
    96→            ),
    97→        )
    98→        conn.commit()
    99→    finally:
   100→        conn.close()
   101→
