Show input payload 
{
  "description": "Write heartbeat module tests",
  "prompt": "Write test files for the Phase 2 heartbeat modules in D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow. Create these test files:\n\n1. `tests/heartbeat/config.test.ts` — tests for src/heartbeat/config.ts\n2. `tests/heartbeat/lock.test.ts` — tests for src/heartbeat/lock.ts  \n3. `tests/heartbeat/alert.test.ts` — tests for src/heartbeat/alert.ts\n4. `tests/heartbeat/log.test.ts` — tests for src/heartbeat/log.ts\n5. `tests/heartbeat/heartbeat.test.ts` — tests for src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts\n\nCONSTRAINTS:\n- ESM project, vitest globals enabled, import from 'vitest'\n- Use `mkdtempSync` for temp dirs, clean up in afterEach with `rmSync`\n- For modules that use `homedir()` or fixed paths, use `vi.mock('node:os')` or `vi.spyOn` to redirect to temp dirs\n- Import using `.js` extensions: `from '../../src/heartbeat/config.js'`\n\n### config.test.ts (~12 tests):\nRead the source first: `D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\src\\heartbeat\\config.ts`\n- loadConfig: default when no file exists, valid TOML, invalid TOML, missing fields use defaults\n- saveConfig: roundtrip (save then load matches), projects serialization\n- intervalToSeconds: '5m'→300, '1h'→3600, '30s'→30, invalid→300(default)\n- registerProject: adds new project, skips duplicate (by resolved path)\n- configPath/memorytreeRoot: return correct paths\n\nSince these modules use `homedir()` for paths, the tests need to mock the config path. The simplest approach: \n- Mock the `configPath()` function to return a path in a temp dir\n- OR use `vi.mock('node:os')` to override `homedir()`\n\nActually, the cleanest approach for config.ts: just test intervalToSeconds and registerProject directly (they don't use filesystem). For loadConfig/saveConfig, mock `configPath()` to return a temp path.\n\n### lock.test.ts (~8 tests):\nRead the source first: `D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\src\\heartbeat\\lock.ts`\n- acquireLock/releaseLock: acquire succeeds, release removes, acquire fails when held by current process\n- readLockPid: returns PID when locked, null when not locked\n- isProcessAlive: current PID is alive, PID 0 is not alive, very large PID is not alive\n- Stale detection: lock file with dead PID gets reclaimed\n\n### alert.test.ts (~10 tests):\nRead the source first: `D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\src\\heartbeat\\alert.ts`\n- writeAlert: creates alert, deduplicates (updates count), caps at MAX_ALERTS\n- writeAlertWithThreshold: doesn't write until threshold reached\n- resetFailureCount: resets counter\n- readAlerts: empty when no file, returns array\n- clearAlerts: removes file\n- formatAlertsForDisplay: formats correctly, handles empty\n\n### log.test.ts (~5 tests):\nRead the source first: `D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\src\\heartbeat\\log.ts`\n- setupLogging: returns Logger, writes to file\n- getLogger: returns same instance\n- Log levels: debug filtered when level is info\n- Log file path includes date\n\n### heartbeat.test.ts (~8 tests):\nRead the source first: `D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\src\\heartbeat\\heartbeat.ts`\n- scanSensitive: detects API keys, passwords, tokens, bearer tokens; no false positives on normal text\n- SENSITIVE_PATTERNS: test each regex pattern individually\n- The main/runHeartbeat/processProject functions need heavy mocking of filesystem and git - keep tests focused on scanSensitive and the patterns\n\nFor the filesystem-dependent tests, the best approach is to:\n1. Create a temp dir\n2. Override the module's path functions using vi.mock or vi.spyOn\n3. Write test files there\n4. Clean up\n\nWRITE all 5 test files using the Write tool. Read the source files first to understand exact function signatures.",
  "run_in_background": true
}
Tool Call #407 Agent toolu_014tbtueMRHspDybqpJGBjvP 09:59:45
Agent input={"description":"Write recall + daemon tests","prompt":"Write test files for the Phase 2 recall and daemon modules in D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow. Create these test files:\n\n...
Show input payload 
{
  "description": "Write recall + daemon tests",
  "prompt": "Write test files for the Phase 2 recall and daemon modules in D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow. Create these test files:\n\n1. `tests/recall/recall.test.ts` — tests for src/recall/recall.ts\n2. `tests/cli/daemon.test.ts` — tests for src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts\n\nCONSTRAINTS:\n- ESM project, vitest globals enabled, import from 'vitest'\n- Use `mkdtempSync` for temp dirs, clean up in afterEach with `rmSync`\n- Import using `.js` extensions: `from '../../src/recall/recall.js'`\n\n### recall.test.ts (~10 tests):\nRead the source first: `D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\src\\recall\\recall.ts`\n- cwdMatches: exact match, subdirectory match, no match, empty cwd\n- findLatestFromJsonl: finds latest session, filters by activation time, returns null when no match\n- formatText: formats found result, formats not-found result\n- syncCurrentProject: mainly tested via integration (mock discoverSourceFiles)\n\nFor findLatestFromJsonl tests, create a temp dir with a sessions.jsonl file containing test data.\n\n### daemon.test.ts (~8 tests):\nRead the source first: `D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\src\\cli\\cmd-daemon.ts`\n- heartbeatScriptPath: returns a path ending with cli.js\n- cmdStatus: outputs platform info (mock readLockPid, configPath)\n- isCronRegistered / isLaunchdRegistered / isSchtasksRegistered: test the detection logic\n- cmdInstall/cmdUninstall: these are hard to test without mocking platform-specific commands, so focus on testing the helper/detection functions\n\nThe daemon tests should be lightweight since most functions call external system commands. Focus on:\n- Testing the logic branches (platform detection)\n- Testing helper functions\n- Mocking subprocess calls with vi.mock\n\nWRITE both test files using the Write tool. Read the source files first to understand exact function signatures.",
  "run_in_background": true
}

## Phase 2 Results

| src/utils/exec.ts | 42 | Subprocess wrapper (execCommand, git) |
| src/heartbeat/config.ts | 205 | TOML config load/save, interval parsing |
| src/heartbeat/lock.ts | 169 | PID-based lock, stale detection |
| src/heartbeat/alert.ts | 215 | alerts.json management, threshold |

| src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts | 208 | Main loop, sensitive scan, git commit+push |
| src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts | 299 | 5 subcommands, 3 platform schedulers |
| src/recall/recall.ts | 282 | Sync, SQLite+JSONL fallback lookup |

| lock.test.ts | 14 |
| alert.test.ts | 17 |
| log.test.ts | 7 |
| heartbeat.test.ts | 13 |
| daemon.test.ts | 9 |
| recall.test.ts | 13 |

### Verification
- npm run typecheck — 0 errors
- npm run lint — 0 errors
- npm test — 301 tests passing (14 test files)
- npm run build — success

Ready to commit when you say the word, then we can move to Phase 3.

<output-file>C:\Users\ai\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\D--demo1-memorytree-workflow\5ae44e4a-fafa-4a58-99cc-a2fa603ea563\tasks\ad33b13d184eabef5.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Implement lock.ts" completed</summary>
<result>Zero diagnostics. The implementation is complete.

---

Summary of D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\src\heartbeat\lock.ts:

The file is a faithful TypeScript port of scripts/_lock_utils.py with five public exports:

| Export | Description |
|---|---|
| lockPath() | Returns ~/.memorytree/heartbeat.lock |
| acquireLock() | Acquires the PID lock with stale/corrupt detection; uses O_CREAT | O_EXCL for race safety |
| releaseLock() | Removes the lock file |
| readLockPid() | Reads the stored PID or returns null |
| isProcessAlive(pid) | Cross-platform liveness check (Unix: process.kill(pid, 0) with EPERM handling; Windows: tasklist /FI "PID eq ...") |

Key implementation choices:
- Uses node:fs constants (O_CREAT | O_EXCL | O_WRONLY) for atomic lock creation, matching the Python os.open approach.
- On Unix, process.kill(pid, 0) mirrors Python's os.kill(pid, 0), with EPERM treated as "alive" (same as Python's PermissionError branch).
- On Windows, execFileSync('tasklist', ...) replaces the Python ctypes.windll.kernel32.OpenProcess approach, as noted in the requirements.
- All imports use .js-free paths since they only import from node: built-ins (no local module imports needed).
- Compiles cleanly under strict: true, exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true, noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true with zero errors and zero VS Code diagnostics.</result>

<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Implement alert.ts" completed</summary>
<result>No errors in alert.ts. The file compiles cleanly under strict: true, exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true, and noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true.

Here is a summary of what was implemented in D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\src\heartbeat\alert.ts:

Exported constants:
- MAX_ALERTS (100) -- cap on stored alerts
- ALERT_TYPES -- ReadonlySet<string> with no_remote, sensitive_match, push_failed, lock_held
- FAILURE_THRESHOLD (3) -- consecutive failures before an alert is persisted

Exported type:
- Alert interface with timestamp, project, type, message, count, plus index signature for flexibility

Exported functions (faithful port of each Python counterpart):
- alertsPath() -- resolves ~/.memorytree/alerts.json
- readAlerts() -- reads and parses the alerts file, returning [] on missing/corrupt data
- writeAlert(project, alertType, message) -- appends or dedup-updates an alert, trims to MAX_ALERTS
- writeAlertWithThreshold(project, alertType, message) -- increments failure count; only writes alert when threshold is reached
- resetFailureCount(project, alertType) -- removes a failure counter (immutable object rebuild)
- clearAlerts() -- deletes the alerts file
- formatAlertsForDisplay(alerts) -- renders alerts as indented human-readable lines

Design decisions matching project conventions:
- Self-contained UTC timestamp helper (utcTimestamp()) to avoid cross-module dependency
- Immutable patterns throughout (spread operator for object updates, readonly arrays)
- No .js extension imports needed since the file has no local imports
- node:fs, node:os, node:path used consistently with the existing config.ts style</result>

<task-notification>

<tool-use-id>toolu_01JXT33zPwawjpE5GXCi5bFX</tool-use-id>
<output-file>C:\Users\ai\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\D--demo1-memorytree-workflow\5ae44e4a-fafa-4a58-99cc-a2fa603ea563\tasks\a3447f5648dd57ba4.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Implement log.ts" completed</summary>
<result>Zero diagnostics for log.ts. The file compiles cleanly under the project's strict settings.

Here is a summary of what was implemented in D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\src\heartbeat\log.ts:

Exported types:
- LogLevel -- union type 'debug' | 'info' | 'warn' | 'error'
- Logger -- interface with debug, info, warn, error, and exception methods

Exported functions:
- setupLogging(logLevel?) -- creates a singleton MemoryTreeLogger; only the first call's logLevel takes effect (mirrors Python's "if logger.handlers: return" guard)
- getLogger() -- returns the singleton, auto-creating with 'info' level if not yet set up (convenient default rather than throwing)
- _resetLogger() -- resets the singleton for test isolation

MemoryTreeLogger class (internal):
- Stores level as numeric priority (debug=0, info=1, warn=2, error=3); each log method checks LEVEL_PRIORITY[level] < this.levelPriority before writing
- Writes to process.stderr.write() and appendFileSync() -- both Windows-safe for concurrent access
- Log file path: ~/.memorytree/logs/heartbeat-YYYY-MM-DD.log (UTC date, matching Python)
- Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS [LEVEL] message (UTC timestamps, matching Python's datefmt)
- exception() appends err.stack if the error is an Error instance, otherwise String(err)
- If the log directory cannot be created, falls back to stderr-only mode with a warning (mirrors Python's except OSError)

Helpers:
- resolveLevel() -- maps strings to LogLevel, including 'warning' -> 'warn' alias, defaulting to 'info'
- logFilePathForToday() -- builds the date-stamped log file path
- timestamp() -- formats UTC time as YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS

The two pre-existing errors in heartbeat.ts (lines 40 and 77) are unrelated to this change. One of them (Argument of type 'string' is not assignable to parameter of type 'LogLevel | undefined') is actually caused by heartbeat.ts passing an untyped string to the now-properly-typed setupLogging -- that file will need a minor cast or type narrowing when it is ported.</result>

<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Write heartbeat module tests" completed</summary>
<result>All 69 tests across all 5 test files pass. Here is a summary of what was created:

Test files created (69 tests total, all passing):

1. D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\tests\heartbeat\config.test.ts (18 tests)
- configPath / memorytreeRoot (2): correct path structure
- loadConfig (4): defaults when no file, valid TOML parsing, invalid TOML fallback, missing fields use defaults
- saveConfig (2): roundtrip save/load, empty projects serialization
- intervalToSeconds (7): 5m->300, 1h->3600, 30s->30, invalid->300 default, 0m->300, whitespace handling, case-insensitivity
- registerProject (3): adds new project, skips duplicate, does not mutate original config

2. D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\tests\heartbeat\lock.test.ts (14 tests)
- lockPath (1): correct path
- acquireLock / releaseLock (4): succeed when free, fail when held, release removes file, re-acquire after release
- readLockPid (3): returns PID when locked, null when no file, null for corrupt
- isProcessAlive (4): current PID alive, PID 0 not alive, negative PID not alive, large PID not alive
- stale lock detection (2): reclaims dead PID lock, reclaims corrupt lock

3. D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\tests\heartbeat\alert.test.ts (17 tests)
- alertsPath (1): correct path
- readAlerts (1): empty when no file
- writeAlert (5): creates alert, deduplicates with count update, different types not merged, different projects not merged, caps at MAX_ALERTS
- writeAlertWithThreshold (2): no write below threshold, writes after threshold
- resetFailureCount (2): resets counter, no-op for nonexistent key
- clearAlerts (2): removes all alerts, no-op when no file
- formatAlertsForDisplay (4): empty string for empty array, single alert formatting, count suffix for count>1, multiple alerts on separate lines

4. D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\tests\heartbeat\log.test.ts (7 tests)
- setupLogging (2): returns Logger with all methods, writes to file
- getLogger (2): returns same singleton, auto-creates if not set up
- log level filtering (2): debug filtered at info level, debug visible at debug level
- log file naming (1): file name includes YYYY-MM-DD date

5. D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\tests\heartbeat\heartbeat.test.ts (13 tests)
- scanSensitive (8): detects API keys, passwords, tokens, known prefixes (sk-, ghp_), Bearer tokens, no false positives on normal text, stops after first match
- SENSITIVE_PATTERNS individual patterns (5): api_key variants, password/passwd/pwd, secret/token, known prefixes (sk-, pk_live_, sk_live_, ghp_, gho_, glpat-), Bearer token length requirements

Approach used:
- vi.mock('node:os') to override homedir() and redirect all filesystem operations (config, lock, alerts, logs) to mkdtempSync temp directories
- Each test file creates a fresh temp dir in beforeEach and cleans up with rmSync in afterEach
- The log tests use _resetLogger() to clear the singleton between tests
- ESM imports with .js extensions, vitest globals from 'vitest'</result>

{
  "command": "cd D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow && git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfeat: implement heartbeat, daemon, and recall modules (Phase 2)\n\nPort 8 Python modules to TypeScript with 91 new tests (301 total):\n- exec.ts: subprocess wrapper with allowFailure support\n- config.ts: TOML config load/save, interval parsing, project registration\n- lock.ts: PID-based lock with stale detection, cross-platform isProcessAlive\n- alert.ts: alerts.json management, threshold-based writes, dedup\n- log.ts: Logger class with file+stderr dual output, daily rotation\n- heartbeat.ts: main loop, per-project processing, sensitive pattern scan, git commit+push\n- cmd-daemon.ts: install/uninstall/run-once/watch/status, 3 platform schedulers\n- recall.ts: on-demand sync, SQLite+JSONL fallback session lookup\n\nAll verifications pass: typecheck 0 errors, lint 0 errors, 301 tests, build OK.\nEOF\n)\" && git push",
  "description": "Commit Phase 2 and push"
}

1→from __future__ import annotations
     2→
     3→import re
     4→from dataclasses import dataclass
     5→from datetime import datetime
     6→from pathlib import Path
     7→
     8→from _locale_utils import normalize_locale
     9→
    10→
    11→DIRS = [
    12→    "Memory/01_goals",
    13→    "Memory/02_todos",
    14→    "Memory/03_chat_logs",
    15→    "Memory/04_knowledge",
    16→    "Memory/05_archive",
    17→]
    18→
    19→CONTENT_POLICY_SOURCE_PATTERNS = [
    20→    "CONTRIBUTING",
    21→    "CONTRIBUTING.md",
    22→    "CONTRIBUTING.*",
    23→    ".github/CONTRIBUTING",
    24→    ".github/CONTRIBUTING.*",
    25→    ".github/CONTRIBUTING/*.md",
    26→    "docs/CONTRIBUTING*.md",
    27→    "docs/**/CONTRIBUTING*.md",
    28→]
    29→
    30→ALWAYS_POLICY_SOURCE_PATTERNS = [
    31→    "CODEOWNERS",
    32→    ".github/CODEOWNERS",
    33→    ".github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md",
    34→    ".github/pull_request_template.md",
    35→    ".github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/*.md",
    36→    ".github/pull_request_template/*.md",
    37→    ".gitlab/merge_request_templates/*.md",
    38→    ".commitlintrc",
    39→    ".commitlintrc.json",
    40→    ".commitlintrc.yaml",
    41→    ".commitlintrc.yml",
    42→    ".commitlintrc.js",
    43→    ".commitlintrc.cjs",
    44→    "commitlint.config.js",
    45→    "commitlint.config.cjs",
    46→    "commitlint.config.mjs",
    47→    ".pre-commit-config.yaml",
    48→    ".pre-commit-config.yml",
    49→]
    50→
    51→POLICY_TEXT_PATTERNS = (
    52→    r"\bpull request\b",
    53→    r"\bmerge request\b",
    54→    r"\bpr\b",
    55→    r"\bbranch(?:es)?\b",
    56→    r"\bprotected branch(?:es)?\b",
    57→    r"\btarget branch\b",
    58→    r"\bbase branch\b",
    59→    r"\bcommit(?: message)?\b",
    60→    r"\bconventional commit(?:s)?\b",
    61→    r"\bcommitlint\b",
    62→    r"\breview(?:er|ers)?\b",
    63→    r"\bapproval(?:s)?\b",
    64→    r"\bci\b",
    65→    r"\be2e\b",
    66→    r"\bpipeline(?:s)?\b",
    67→    r"\bauto-merge\b",
    68→    r"\bsquash merge\b",
    69→    r"\bpre-push\b",
    70→    r"\bpre-commit\b",
    71→    r"\bcodeowners\b",
    72→    r"\bstatus checks?\b",
    73→    r"\brequired checks?\b",
    74→    r"\brequired approvals?\b",
    75→    r"\bmerge strategy\b",
    76→    r"\u62c9\u53d6\u8bf7\u6c42",
    77→    r"\u5408\u5e76\u8bf7\u6c42",
    78→    r"\u5206\u652f",
    79→    r"\u63d0\u4ea4\u4fe1\u606f",
    80→    r"\u63d0\u4ea4\u6807\u9898",
    81→    r"\u8bc4\u5ba1",
    82→    r"\u5ba1\u6279",
    83→    r"\u6d41\u6c34\u7ebf",
    84→    r"\u68c0\u67e5",
    85→    r"\u81ea\u52a8\u5408\u5e76",
    86→    r"\u63d0\u4ea4\u89c4\u8303",
    87→    r"\u9884\u63a8\u9001",
    88→    r"\u9884\u63d0\u4ea4",
    89→)
    90→
    91→
    92→def build_datetime(date_value: str, time_value: str) -> datetime:
    93→    if date_value and time_value:
    94→        return datetime.strptime(f"{date_value} {time_value}", "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")
    95→    if date_value:
    96→        return datetime.strptime(f"{date_value} 00:00", "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")
    97→    if time_value:
    98→        today = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
    99→        return datetime.strptime(f"{today} {time_value}", "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")
   100→    return datetime.now()
   101→
   102→
   103→def resolve_template_dir(skill_root: Path, root: Path, locale_value: str) -> Path:
   104→    templates_root = skill_root / "assets" / "templates"
   105→    locale_name = normalize_locale(locale_value, root)
   106→    template_dir = templates_root / locale_name
   107→    if not template_dir.exists():
   108→        raise SystemExit(f"unsupported locale: {locale_value}")
   109→    return template_dir
   110→
   111→
   112→def find_external_policy_sources(root: Path) -> list[str]:
   113→    matches: set[str] = set()
   114→    for pattern in ALWAYS_POLICY_SOURCE_PATTERNS:
   115→        for path in root.glob(pattern):
   116→            if path.is_file():
   117→                matches.add(path.relative_to(root).as_posix())
   118→    for pattern in CONTENT_POLICY_SOURCE_PATTERNS:
   119→        for path in root.glob(pattern):
   120→            if path.is_file() and has_policy_content(path):
   121→                matches.add(path.relative_to(root).as_posix())
   122→    return sorted(matches)
   123→
   124→
   125→def has_policy_content(path: Path) -> bool:
   126→    try:
   127→        text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore").lower()
   128→    except OSError:
   129→        return True
   130→    return any(re.search(pattern, text) for pattern in POLICY_TEXT_PATTERNS)
   131→
   132→
   133→def find_latest_version(folder: Path, pattern: str) -> str | None:
   134→    regex = re.compile(pattern)
   135→    versions = []
   136→    if folder.exists():
   137→        for path in folder.iterdir():
   138→            match = regex.fullmatch(path.name)
   139→            if match:
   140→                versions.append(match.group(1))
   141→    return max(versions) if versions else None
   142→
   143→
   144→def find_latest_goal_file(folder: Path) -> Path | None:
   145→    regex = re.compile(r"goal_v(\d{3})_(\d{8})\.md")
   146→    matches: list[tuple[str, str, Path]] = []
   147→    if folder.exists():
   148→        for path in folder.iterdir():
   149→            match = regex.fullmatch(path.name)
   150→            if match:
   151→                matches.append((match.group(1), match.group(2), path))
   152→    if not matches:
   153→        return None
   154→    matches.sort()
   155→    return matches[-1][2]
   156→
   157→
   158→def find_latest_todo_version(folder: Path, goal_version: str) -> str | None:
   159→    regex = re.compile(rf"todo_v{goal_version}_(\d{{3}})_\d{{8}}\.md")
   160→    versions = []
   161→    if folder.exists():
   162→        for path in folder.iterdir():
   163→            match = regex.fullmatch(path.name)
   164→            if match:
   165→                versions.append(match.group(1))
   166→    return max(versions) if versions else None
   167→
   168→
   169→def find_latest_todo_file(folder: Path, goal_version: str) -> Path | None:
   170→    regex = re.compile(rf"todo_v{goal_version}_(\d{{3}})_(\d{{8}})\.md")
   171→    matches: list[tuple[str, str, Path]] = []
   172→    if folder.exists():
   173→        for path in folder.iterdir():
   174→            match = regex.fullmatch(path.name)
   175→            if match:
   176→                matches.append((match.group(1), match.group(2), path))
   177→    if not matches:
   178→        return None
   179→    matches.sort()
   180→    return matches[-1][2]
   181→
   182→
   183→def find_latest_chat_log_file(folder: Path) -> Path | None:
   184→    regex = re.compile(r"(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})_(\d{2}-\d{2})\.md")
   185→    matches: list[tuple[str, str, Path]] = []
   186→    if folder.exists():
   187→        for path in folder.iterdir():
   188→            match = regex.fullmatch(path.name)
   189→            if match:
   190→                matches.append((match.group(1), match.group(2), path))
   191→    if not matches:
   192→        return None
   193→    matches.sort()
   194→    return matches[-1][2]
   195→
   196→
   197→def write_template(template_path: Path, target_path: Path, force: bool, values: dict[str, str]) -> bool:
   198→    if target_path.exists() and not force:
   199→        return False
   200→    content = template_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   201→    for key, value in values.items():
   202→        content = content.replace(f"{{{{{key}}}}}", value)
   203→    target_path.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
   204→    return True
   205→
   206→
   207→@dataclass
   208→class ScaffoldPaths:
   209→    root: Path
   210→    goal_dir: Path
   211→    todo_dir: Path
   212→    chat_dir: Path
   213→    goal_path: Path
   214→    todo_path: Path
   215→    chat_path: Path
   216→    goal_version: str
   217→    todo_version: str
   218→    previous_version: str
   219→    date_label: str
   220→    time_label: str
   221→
   222→
   223→def resolve_scaffold_paths(root: Path, dt: datetime) -> ScaffoldPaths:
   224→    date_token = dt.strftime("%Y%m%d")
   225→    date_label = dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
   226→    time_label = dt.strftime("%H:%M")
   227→    chat_filename = dt.strftime("%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M.md")
   228→
   229→    goal_dir = root / "Memory" / "01_goals"
   230→    todo_dir = root / "Memory" / "02_todos"
   231→    chat_dir = root / "Memory" / "03_chat_logs"
   232→
   233→    goal_version = find_latest_version(goal_dir, r"goal_v(\d{3})_\d{8}\.md")
   234→    if goal_version is None:
   235→        goal_version = "001"
   236→    previous_version = f"v{int(goal_version) - 1:03d}" if int(goal_version) > 1 else "none"
   237→
   238→    todo_version = find_latest_todo_version(todo_dir, goal_version)
   239→    if todo_version is None:
   240→        todo_version = "001"
   241→
   242→    goal_path = find_latest_goal_file(goal_dir) or goal_dir / f"goal_v{goal_version}_{date_token}.md"
   243→    todo_path = (
   244→        find_latest_todo_file(todo_dir, goal_version)
   245→        or todo_dir / f"todo_v{goal_version}_{todo_version}_{date_token}.md"
   246→    )
   247→    chat_path = find_latest_chat_log_file(chat_dir) or chat_dir / chat_filename
   248→
   249→    return ScaffoldPaths(
   250→        root=root,
   251→        goal_dir=goal_dir,
   252→        todo_dir=todo_dir,
   253→        chat_dir=chat_dir,
   254→        goal_path=goal_path,
   255→        todo_path=todo_path,
   256→        chat_path=chat_path,
   257→        goal_version=goal_version,
   258→        todo_version=todo_version,
   259→        previous_version=previous_version,
   260→        date_label=date_label,
   261→        time_label=time_label,
   262→    )
   263→
   264→
   265→def create_memory_dirs(root: Path) -> list[str]:
   266→    created: list[str] = []
   267→    for rel in DIRS:
   268→        path = root / rel
   269→        if not path.exists():
   270→            created.append(path.relative_to(root).as_posix())
   271→        path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   272→    return created
   273→
   274→
   275→def scaffold_content_files(
   276→    paths: ScaffoldPaths,
   277→    templates: Path,
   278→    goal_summary: str,
   279→    project_name: str,
   280→    force: bool,
   281→) -> tuple[list[str], list[str]]:
   282→    created: list[str] = []
   283→    preserved: list[str] = []
   284→    pairs = [
   285→        (
   286→            templates / "goal.md",
   287→            paths.goal_path,
   288→            {
   289→                "GOAL_VERSION": paths.goal_version,
   290→                "DATE": paths.date_label,
   291→                "PREVIOUS_VERSION": paths.previous_version,
   292→                "GOAL_SUMMARY": goal_summary.strip(),
   293→            },
   294→        ),
   295→        (
   296→            templates / "todo.md",
   297→            paths.todo_path,
   298→            {
   299→                "GOAL_VERSION": paths.goal_version,
   300→                "TODO_SUBVERSION": paths.todo_version,
   301→                "DATE": paths.date_label,
   302→            },
   303→        ),
   304→        (
   305→            templates / "chat-log.md",
   306→            paths.chat_path,
   307→            {
   308→                "DATE": paths.date_label,
   309→                "TIME": paths.time_label,
   310→                "PROJECT_NAME": project_name.strip(),
   311→            },
   312→        ),
   313→    ]
   314→    for template_path, target_path, values in pairs:
   315→        if write_template(template_path, target_path, force, values):
   316→            created.append(target_path.relative_to(paths.root).as_posix())
   317→        else:
   318→            preserved.append(target_path.relative_to(paths.root).as_posix())
   319→    return created, preserved
   320→

Tool Result #465 Read toolu_01DjBZX4gR9GX8xjTXBcr4yU 10:13:15
Read output=1→#!/usr/bin/env python3 2→""" 3→Upgrade a repository to MemoryTree without overwriting existing repo policy files. 4→""" 5→ 6→from __future__ import annotations 7→ 8→import arg...
Show output payload 
1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→Upgrade a repository to MemoryTree without overwriting existing repo policy files.
     4→"""
     5→
     6→from __future__ import annotations
     7→
     8→import argparse
     9→import json
    10→import re
    11→from pathlib import Path
    12→
    13→from _locale_utils import normalize_locale
    14→from _scaffold_utils import (
    15→    build_datetime,
    16→    create_memory_dirs,
    17→    find_external_policy_sources,
    18→    find_latest_chat_log_file,
    19→    find_latest_goal_file,
    20→    find_latest_todo_file,
    21→    resolve_scaffold_paths,
    22→    resolve_template_dir,
    23→    scaffold_content_files,
    24→    write_template,
    25→)
    26→
    27→
    28→def main() -> int:
    29→    args = parse_args()
    30→    root = Path(args.root).resolve()
    31→    if not root.exists():
    32→        raise SystemExit(f"root does not exist: {root}")
    33→
    34→    skill_root = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
    35→    templates = resolve_template_dir(skill_root, root, args.locale)
    36→    effective_locale = normalize_locale(args.locale, root)
    37→    initial_state = detect_state(root)
    38→
    39→    dt = build_datetime(args.date, args.time)
    40→
    41→    created_dirs = create_memory_dirs(root)
    42→    paths = resolve_scaffold_paths(root, dt)
    43→    created_files, preserved_files = scaffold_content_files(
    44→        paths, templates, args.goal_summary, args.project_name, force=False,
    45→    )
    46→
    47→    agents_path = root / "AGENTS.md"
    48→    agents_action = "preserved_existing"
    49→    if not agents_path.exists():
    50→        if write_template(templates / "agents.md", agents_path, False, {}):
    51→            created_files.append("AGENTS.md")
    52→            agents_action = "created"
    53→    else:
    54→        preserved_files.append("AGENTS.md")
    55→    merge_required = agents_action == "preserved_existing" and needs_agents_merge(agents_path)
    56→
    57→    result = {
    58→        "state_before": initial_state,
    59→        "state_after": detect_state(root),
    60→        "requested_locale": args.locale,
    61→        "effective_locale": effective_locale,
    62→        "created_dirs": created_dirs,
    63→        "created_files": created_files,
    64→        "preserved_files": preserved_files,
    65→        "agents_action": agents_action,
    66→        "agents_merge_required": merge_required,
    67→    }
    68→
    69→    if args.format == "json":
    70→        print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False))
    71→    else:
    72→        print(format_result_text(result))
    73→    return 0
    74→
    75→
    76→def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
    77→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Upgrade a repository to MemoryTree safely.")
    78→    parser.add_argument("--root", default=".", help="Target repository root.")
    79→    parser.add_argument("--project-name", default="this project", help="Project name used in generated chat logs.")
    80→    parser.add_argument(
    81→        "--goal-summary",
    82→        default="Describe the long-term project goal here.",
    83→        help="Fallback north-star summary when a goal file must be created.",
    84→    )
    85→    parser.add_argument("--locale", default="auto", help="Requested locale: auto, en, or zh-cn.")
    86→    parser.add_argument("--date", default="", help="Override date as YYYY-MM-DD for deterministic runs.")
    87→    parser.add_argument(
    88→        "--time",
    89→        default="",
    90→        help="Override time as HH:MM. If --date is omitted, use the current local date.",
    91→    )
    92→    parser.add_argument("--format", choices=("text", "json"), default="text", help="Output format.")
    93→    return parser.parse_args()
    94→
    95→
    96→def detect_state(root: Path) -> str:
    97→    memory_dir = root / "Memory"
    98→    goal_dir = root / "Memory" / "01_goals"
    99→    todo_dir = root / "Memory" / "02_todos"
   100→    chat_dir = root / "Memory" / "03_chat_logs"
   101→    agents_path = root / "AGENTS.md"
   102→    policy_sources = find_external_policy_sources(root)
   103→
   104→    latest_goal = find_latest_goal_file(goal_dir)
   105→    goal_version = extract_goal_version(latest_goal)
   106→    has_goal = latest_goal is not None
   107→    has_todo = goal_version is not None and find_latest_todo_file(todo_dir, goal_version) is not None
   108→    has_chat = find_latest_chat_log_file(chat_dir) is not None
   109→    has_agents = agents_path.is_file()
   110→    agents_ready = has_agents and not needs_agents_merge(agents_path)
   111→
   112→    if not memory_dir.exists():
   113→        return "partial" if has_agents or policy_sources else "not-installed"
   114→    if has_goal and has_todo and has_chat and agents_ready:
   115→        return "installed"
   116→    return "partial"
   117→
   118→
   119→def extract_goal_version(path: Path | None) -> str | None:
   120→    if path is None:
   121→        return None
   122→    match = re.fullmatch(r"goal_v(\d{3})_\d{8}\.md", path.name)
   123→    if match is None:
   124→        return None
   125→    return match.group(1)
   126→
   127→
   128→def needs_agents_merge(path: Path) -> bool:
   129→    try:
   130→        text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore").lower()
   131→    except OSError:
   132→        return True
   133→
   134→    required_checks = [
   135→        has_read_order_signal(text),
   136→        has_memory_layout_signal(text),
   137→        has_active_todo_rule(text),
   138→        has_append_only_chat_log_rule(text),
   139→        has_repo_safety_rule(text),
   140→        has_commit_title_rule(text),
   141→        has_dedicated_memorytree_pr_flow(text),
   142→        has_memorytree_only_scope_rule(text),
   143→        has_auto_merge_rule(text),
   144→    ]
   145→    return not all(required_checks)
   146→
   147→
   148→def matches_any(text: str, patterns: tuple[str, ...]) -> bool:
   149→    return any(re.search(pattern, text) for pattern in patterns)
   150→
   151→
   152→def matches_all_concepts(text: str, concept_groups: tuple[tuple[str, ...], ...]) -> bool:
   153→    return all(matches_any(text, patterns) for patterns in concept_groups)
   154→
   155→
   156→def has_read_order_signal(text: str) -> bool:
   157→    return matches_any(
   158→        text,
   159→        (
   160→            r"\bread order\b",
   161→            r"\bread .* in order\b",
   162→            r"\u8bfb\u53d6\u987a\u5e8f",
   163→            r"\u6309\u6b64\u987a\u5e8f\u8bfb\u53d6",
   164→        ),
   165→    )
   166→
   167→
   168→def has_memory_layout_signal(text: str) -> bool:
   169→    required_paths = (
   170→        (r"memory/01_goals", r"\b01_goals\b"),
   171→        (r"memory/02_todos", r"\b02_todos\b"),
   172→        (r"memory/03_chat_logs", r"\b03_chat_logs\b"),
   173→    )
   174→    return matches_all_concepts(text, required_paths)
   175→
   176→
   177→def has_active_todo_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   178→    return matches_any(
   179→        text,
   180→        (
   181→            r"keep .*todo .*synchron",
   182→            r"todo .*aligned .*active goal",
   183→            r"(sync|align|keep|maintain|bind).{0,50}(active|current).{0,20}todo.{0,50}(active|current).{0,20}goal",
   184→            r"(active|current).{0,20}todo.{0,50}(sync|align|match|bound).{0,50}(active|current).{0,20}goal",
   185→            r"\u5f53\u524d\u5f85\u529e\u5fc5\u987b\u4e0e\u5f53\u524d\u76ee\u6807\u4fdd\u6301\u540c\u6b65",
   186→            r"\u5f85\u529e\u5fc5\u987b\u4e0e\u5f53\u524d\u76ee\u6807\u4fdd\u6301\u540c\u6b65",
   187→        ),
   188→    )
   189→
   190→
   191→def has_append_only_chat_log_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   192→    return matches_any(
   193→        text,
   194→        (
   195→            r"append[- ]only .*chat log",
   196→            r"append[- ]only records",
   197→            r"treat chat logs as append[- ]only",
   198→            r"never rewrite or delete prior entries",
   199→            r"\u5bf9\u8bdd\u65e5\u5fd7\u53ea\u8ffd\u52a0",
   200→            r"\u7981\u6b62\u91cd\u5199\u6216\u5220\u9664\u65e2\u6709\u5185\u5bb9",
   201→        ),
   202→    )
   203→
   204→
   205→def has_repo_safety_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   206→    concept_groups = (
   207→        (r"\bpr\b", r"\bpull request\b", r"\bmerge request\b", r"\u62c9\u53d6\u8bf7\u6c42", r"\u5408\u5e76\u8bf7\u6c42"),
   208→        (r"\bci\b", r"\bchecks?\b", r"\bpipeline\b", r"\be2e\b", r"\u68c0\u67e5", r"\u6d41\u6c34\u7ebf"),
   209→        (r"\breview(?:er|ers)?\b", r"\bapproval(?:s)?\b", r"\u8bc4\u5ba1", r"\u5ba1\u6279"),
   210→        (r"\bbranch(?:es)?\b", r"\bprotected branch(?:es)?\b", r"\u5206\u652f"),
   211→    )
   212→    return matches_all_concepts(text, concept_groups) and matches_any(
   213→        text,
   214→        (
   215→            r"\brepository\b",
   216→            r"\brepo\b",
   217→            r"\bproject\b",
   218→            r"\bhost\b",
   219→            r"\brule(?:s)?\b",
   220→            r"\brequirement(?:s)?\b",
   221→            r"\bpolicy\b",
   222→            r"\bcontrol(?:s)?\b",
   223→            r"\u4ed3\u5e93",
   224→            r"\u89c4\u5219",
   225→            r"\u8981\u6c42",
   226→        ),
   227→    )
   228→
   229→
   230→def has_commit_title_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   231→    return matches_any(
   232→        text,
   233→        (
   234→            r"\bmemorytree\([^)]*\):",
   235→            r"\b[a-z]+\((memorytree)\):",
   236→            r"\bmemorytree:",
   237→            r"memorytree-scoped commit title",
   238→            r"memorytree-specific commit title",
   239→            r"use .*memorytree.*commit title",
   240→            r"\u63d0\u4ea4\u6807\u9898.*memorytree",
   241→        ),
   242→    )
   243→
   244→
   245→def has_dedicated_memorytree_pr_flow(text: str) -> bool:
   246→    concept_groups = (
   247→        (r"\bmemorytree\b", r"\u8bb0\u5fc6"),
   248→        (r"\bbranch(?:es)?\b", r"\u5206\u652f"),
   249→        (r"\bpr\b", r"\bpull request\b", r"\bmerge request\b", r"\u62c9\u53d6\u8bf7\u6c42", r"\u5408\u5e76\u8bf7\u6c42"),
   250→        (r"\bdedicated\b", r"\bseparate\b", r"\bisolated\b", r"\bown\b", r"\u4e13\u7528", r"\u72ec\u7acb"),
   251→    )
   252→    return matches_all_concepts(text, concept_groups)
   253→
   254→
   255→def has_memorytree_only_scope_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   256→    return matches_any(
   257→        text,
   258→        (
   259→            r"memorytree[- ]owned changes",
   260→            r"memorytree[- ]owned files",
   261→            r"memorytree-managed files",
   262→            r"(stage|push|commit).{0,40}only.{0,40}memorytree[- ]owned.{0,20}(files|changes)?",
   263→            r"(stage|push|commit).{0,40}only.{0,40}memorytree.{0,20}(files|changes|diff)",
   264→            r"only.{0,40}memorytree.{0,20}(files|changes|diff)",
   265→            r"\u4ec5\u81ea\u52a8\u63d0\u4ea4\u548c\u63a8\u9001.*memorytree.*\u53d8\u66f4",
   266→            r"\u7531 memorytree \u7ba1\u7406",
   267→        ),
   268→    )
   269→
   270→
   271→def has_auto_merge_rule(text: str) -> bool:
   272→    return matches_any(
   273→        text,
   274→        (
   275→            r"auto-merge only when .*permit",
   276→            r"only when repository rules permit",
   277→            r"only when repo rules permit",
   278→            r"only enable auto-merge when.{0,60}(required )?(approvals|checks|reviews?)",
   279→            r"auto-merge.{0,60}(required approvals|required checks|repository rules)",
   280→            r"\u4ec5\u5728\u4ed3\u5e93\u89c4\u5219\u5141\u8bb8\u65f6\u624d\u5f00\u542f\u81ea\u52a8\u5408\u5e76",
   281→        ),
   282→    )
   283→
   284→
   285→def format_result_text(result: dict) -> str:
   286→    lines = [
   287→        f"state_before: {result['state_before']}",
   288→        f"state_after: {result['state_after']}",
   289→        f"effective_locale: {result['effective_locale']}",
   290→        f"agents_action: {result['agents_action']}",
   291→        f"agents_merge_required: {str(result['agents_merge_required']).lower()}",
   292→    ]
   293→    if result["created_dirs"]:
   294→        lines.append("created_dirs:")
   295→        lines.extend(f"- {item}" for item in result["created_dirs"])
   296→    if result["created_files"]:
   297→        lines.append("created_files:")
   298→        lines.extend(f"- {item}" for item in result["created_files"])
   299→    if result["preserved_files"]:
   300→        lines.append("preserved_files:")
   301→        lines.extend(f"- {item}" for item in result["preserved_files"])
   302→    return "\n".join(lines)
   303→
   304→
   305→if __name__ == "__main__":
   306→    raise SystemExit(main())
   307→

Read output=1→from __future__ import annotations 2→ 3→import locale 4→import os 5→import re 6→from pathlib import Path 7→ 8→ 9→TEXT_EXTENSIONS = {".md", ".txt"} 10→CJK_RE = re.compile(r"[\u...
Show output payload 
1→from __future__ import annotations
     2→
     3→import locale
     4→import os
     5→import re
     6→from pathlib import Path
     7→
     8→
     9→TEXT_EXTENSIONS = {".md", ".txt"}
    10→CJK_RE = re.compile(r"[\u3400-\u4dbf\u4e00-\u9fff]")
    11→LATIN_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z]")
    12→MIN_LATIN_SIGNAL = 3
    13→
    14→
    15→def normalize_locale(value: str, root: Path | None = None) -> str:
    16→    normalized = str(value or "").strip().lower()
    17→    if normalized in ("", "auto"):
    18→        normalized = detect_repo_locale(root) or detect_system_locale()
    19→
    20→    mapping = {
    21→        "en": "en",
    22→        "en-us": "en",
    23→        "en-gb": "en",
    24→        "zh": "zh-cn",
    25→        "zh-cn": "zh-cn",
    26→        "zh-sg": "zh-cn",
    27→        "zh-hans": "zh-cn",
    28→        "zh-hant": "zh-cn",
    29→        "zh-tw": "zh-cn",
    30→        "zh-hk": "zh-cn",
    31→    }
    32→    return mapping.get(normalized, normalized)
    33→
    34→
    35→def detect_repo_locale(root: Path | None) -> str | None:
    36→    if root is None or not root.exists():
    37→        return None
    38→
    39→    zh_score = 0
    40→    en_score = 0
    41→
    42→    for path in iter_repo_text_candidates(root):
    43→        name = path.name.lower()
    44→        if "zh-cn" in name or "zh_cn" in name:
    45→            zh_score += 12
    46→
    47→        sample = read_text_sample(path)
    48→        if not sample:
    49→            continue
    50→
    51→        cjk_count = len(CJK_RE.findall(sample))
    52→        latin_count = len(LATIN_RE.findall(sample))
    53→
    54→        if cjk_count >= 8 and cjk_count >= max(4, latin_count // 4):
    55→            zh_score += cjk_count
    56→        if cjk_count == 0 and latin_count >= MIN_LATIN_SIGNAL:
    57→            en_score += max(latin_count, 12)
    58→            continue
    59→        if latin_count >= 40 and latin_count >= max(40, cjk_count * 4):
    60→            en_score += latin_count
    61→
    62→    if zh_score == 0 and en_score == 0:
    63→        return None
    64→    if zh_score == 0:
    65→        return "en"
    66→    if en_score == 0:
    67→        return "zh-cn"
    68→    if zh_score >= en_score * 1.25:
    69→        return "zh-cn"
    70→    if en_score >= zh_score * 1.25:
    71→        return "en"
    72→    return None
    73→
    74→
    75→def detect_system_locale() -> str:
    76→    candidates = [
    77→        locale.getlocale()[0],
    78→        os.environ.get("LC_ALL"),
    79→        os.environ.get("LANG"),
    80→    ]
    81→    for candidate in candidates:
    82→        text = str(candidate or "").lower()
    83→        if text.startswith("zh") or "chinese" in text:
    84→            return "zh-cn"
    85→    return "en"
    86→
    87→
    88→def iter_repo_text_candidates(root: Path):
    89→    seen: set[Path] = set()
    90→
    91→    for rel in ("AGENTS.md", "README.md", "README.zh-CN.md", "README.zh_CN.md"):
    92→        path = root / rel
    93→        if path.is_file():
    94→            seen.add(path)
    95→            yield path
    96→
    97→    top_level = sorted(
    98→        path
    99→        for path in root.iterdir()
   100→        if path.is_file() and path.suffix.lower() in TEXT_EXTENSIONS and path not in seen
   101→    )
   102→    for path in top_level[:8]:
   103→        seen.add(path)
   104→        yield path
   105→
   106→    docs_dir = root / "docs"
   107→    if docs_dir.is_dir():
   108→        count = 0
   109→        for path in sorted(docs_dir.rglob("*")):
   110→            if count >= 12:
   111→                break
   112→            if not path.is_file() or path.suffix.lower() not in TEXT_EXTENSIONS or path in seen:
   113→                continue
   114→            seen.add(path)
   115→            count += 1
   116→            yield path
   117→
   118→
   119→def read_text_sample(path: Path) -> str:
   120→    try:
   121→        return path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")[:4096]
   122→    except OSError:
   123→        return ""
   124→

Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\src\\project\\locale.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * Locale — project language detection.\n * Port of scripts/_locale_utils.py\n */\n\nimport { existsSync, readdirSync, readFileSync, statSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport { join, extname } from 'node:path'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Constants\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nconst TEXT_EXTENSIONS: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set(['.md', '.txt'])\nconst CJK_RE = /[\\u3400-\\u4dbf\\u4e00-\\u9fff]/g\nconst LATIN_RE = /[A-Za-z]/g\nconst MIN_LATIN_SIGNAL = 3\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Public API\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function normalizeLocale(value: string, root?: string): string {\n  let normalized = (value ?? '').trim().toLowerCase()\n  if (normalized === '' || normalized === 'auto') {\n    normalized = (root ? detectRepoLocale(root) : null) ?? detectSystemLocale()\n  }\n\n  const mapping: Record<string, string> = {\n    'en': 'en',\n    'en-us': 'en',\n    'en-gb': 'en',\n    'zh': 'zh-cn',\n    'zh-cn': 'zh-cn',\n    'zh-sg': 'zh-cn',\n    'zh-hans': 'zh-cn',\n    'zh-hant': 'zh-cn',\n    'zh-tw': 'zh-cn',\n    'zh-hk': 'zh-cn',\n  }\n  return mapping[normalized] ?? normalized\n}\n\nexport function detectRepoLocale(root: string): string | null {\n  if (!existsSync(root)) return null\n\n  let zhScore = 0\n  let enScore = 0\n\n  for (const filePath of iterRepoTextCandidates(root)) {\n    const name = filePath.split(/[/\\\\]/).pop()?.toLowerCase() ?? ''\n    if (name.includes('zh-cn') || name.includes('zh_cn')) {\n      zhScore += 12\n    }\n\n    const sample = readTextSample(filePath)\n    if (!sample) continue\n\n    const cjkCount = (sample.match(CJK_RE) ?? []).length\n    const latinCount = (sample.match(LATIN_RE) ?? []).length\n\n    if (cjkCount >= 8 && cjkCount >= Math.max(4, Math.floor(latinCount / 4))) {\n      zhScore += cjkCount\n    }\n    if (cjkCount === 0 && latinCount >= MIN_LATIN_SIGNAL) {\n      enScore += Math.max(latinCount, 12)\n      continue\n    }\n    if (latinCount >= 40 && latinCount >= Math.max(40, cjkCount * 4)) {\n      enScore += latinCount\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (zhScore === 0 && enScore === 0) return null\n  if (zhScore === 0) return 'en'\n  if (enScore === 0) return 'zh-cn'\n  if (zhScore >= enScore * 1.25) return 'zh-cn'\n  if (enScore >= zhScore * 1.25) return 'en'\n  return null\n}\n\nexport function detectSystemLocale(): string {\n  const candidates = [\n    process.env['LC_ALL'],\n    process.env['LANG'],\n    process.env['LANGUAGE'],\n  ]\n  for (const candidate of candidates) {\n    const text = (candidate ?? '').toLowerCase()\n    if (text.startsWith('zh') || text.includes('chinese')) {\n      return 'zh-cn'\n    }\n  }\n  return 'en'\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Internal helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction* iterRepoTextCandidates(root: string): Generator<string> {\n  const seen = new Set<string>()\n\n  for (const rel of ['AGENTS.md', 'README.md', 'README.zh-CN.md', 'README.zh_CN.md']) {\n    const p = join(root, rel)\n    if (existsSync(p) && isFile(p)) {\n      seen.add(p)\n      yield p\n    }\n  }\n\n  const topLevel: string[] = []\n  try {\n    for (const entry of readdirSync(root)) {\n      const p = join(root, entry)\n      if (isFile(p) && TEXT_EXTENSIONS.has(extname(p).toLowerCase()) && !seen.has(p)) {\n        topLevel.push(p)\n      }\n    }\n  } catch {\n    // ignore\n  }\n  topLevel.sort()\n  for (const p of topLevel.slice(0, 8)) {\n    seen.add(p)\n    yield p\n  }\n\n  const docsDir = join(root, 'docs')\n  if (existsSync(docsDir) && isDir(docsDir)) {\n    let count = 0\n    for (const p of walkDir(docsDir)) {\n      if (count >= 12) break\n      if (!isFile(p) || !TEXT_EXTENSIONS.has(extname(p).toLowerCase()) || seen.has(p)) continue\n      seen.add(p)\n      count++\n      yield p\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nfunction walkDir(dir: string): string[] {\n  const results: string[] = []\n  try {\n    for (const entry of readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true })) {\n      const p = join(dir, entry.name)\n      if (entry.isDirectory()) {\n        results.push(...walkDir(p))\n      } else if (entry.isFile()) {\n        results.push(p)\n      }\n    }\n  } catch {\n    // ignore\n  }\n  results.sort()\n  return results\n}\n\nfunction readTextSample(filePath: string): string {\n  try {\n    const buf = Buffer.alloc(4096)\n    const fd = require('node:fs').openSync(filePath, 'r')\n    const bytesRead = require('node:fs').readSync(fd, buf, 0, 4096, 0)\n    require('node:fs').closeSync(fd)\n    return buf.subarray(0, bytesRead).toString('utf-8')\n  } catch {\n    return ''\n  }\n}\n\nfunction isFile(p: string): boolean {\n  try { return statSync(p).isFile() } catch { return false }\n}\n\nfunction isDir(p: string): boolean {\n  try { return statSync(p).isDirectory() } catch { return false }\n}\n"
}

Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\src\\project\\scaffold.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * Scaffold — directory init + template rendering.\n * Port of scripts/_scaffold_utils.py\n */\n\nimport { existsSync, mkdirSync, readdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport { join, relative } from 'node:path'\n\nimport { toPosixPath } from '../utils/path.js'\nimport { normalizeLocale } from './locale.js'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Constants\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport const MEMORY_DIRS: readonly string[] = [\n  'Memory/01_goals',\n  'Memory/02_todos',\n  'Memory/03_chat_logs',\n  'Memory/04_knowledge',\n  'Memory/05_archive',\n]\n\nexport const CONTENT_POLICY_SOURCE_PATTERNS: readonly string[] = [\n  'CONTRIBUTING',\n  'CONTRIBUTING.md',\n]\n\nexport const ALWAYS_POLICY_SOURCE_PATTERNS: readonly string[] = [\n  'CODEOWNERS',\n  '.github/CODEOWNERS',\n  '.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md',\n  '.github/pull_request_template.md',\n  '.commitlintrc',\n  '.commitlintrc.json',\n  '.commitlintrc.yaml',\n  '.commitlintrc.yml',\n  '.commitlintrc.js',\n  '.commitlintrc.cjs',\n  'commitlint.config.js',\n  'commitlint.config.cjs',\n  'commitlint.config.mjs',\n  '.pre-commit-config.yaml',\n  '.pre-commit-config.yml',\n]\n\nconst POLICY_TEXT_PATTERNS: readonly RegExp[] = [\n  /\\bpull request\\b/i, /\\bmerge request\\b/i, /\\bpr\\b/i,\n  /\\bbranch(?:es)?\\b/i, /\\bprotected branch(?:es)?\\b/i,\n  /\\bcommit(?: message)?\\b/i, /\\bconventional commit(?:s)?\\b/i,\n  /\\breview(?:er|ers)?\\b/i, /\\bapproval(?:s)?\\b/i,\n  /\\bci\\b/i, /\\bpipeline(?:s)?\\b/i, /\\bauto-merge\\b/i,\n  /\\bpre-commit\\b/i, /\\bcodeowners\\b/i,\n  /拉取请求/, /合并请求/, /分支/, /提交信息/, /提交标题/,\n  /评审/, /审批/, /流水线/, /检查/, /自动合并/, /提交规范/,\n]\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Types\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport interface ScaffoldPaths {\n  readonly root: string\n  readonly goalDir: string\n  readonly todoDir: string\n  readonly chatDir: string\n  readonly goalPath: string\n  readonly todoPath: string\n  readonly chatPath: string\n  readonly goalVersion: string\n  readonly todoVersion: string\n  readonly previousVersion: string\n  readonly dateLabel: string\n  readonly timeLabel: string\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DateTime helper\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function buildDatetime(dateValue: string, timeValue: string): Date {\n  if (dateValue && timeValue) {\n    return new Date(`${dateValue}T${timeValue}:00`)\n  }\n  if (dateValue) {\n    return new Date(`${dateValue}T00:00:00`)\n  }\n  if (timeValue) {\n    const today = new Date()\n    const y = today.getFullYear()\n    const m = String(today.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0')\n    const d = String(today.getDate()).padStart(2, '0')\n    return new Date(`${y}-${m}-${d}T${timeValue}:00`)\n  }\n  return new Date()\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Template resolution\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function resolveTemplateDir(skillRoot: string, root: string, localeValue: string): string {\n  const templatesRoot = join(skillRoot, 'assets', 'templates')\n  const localeName = normalizeLocale(localeValue, root)\n  const templateDir = join(templatesRoot, localeName)\n  if (!existsSync(templateDir)) {\n    throw new Error(`unsupported locale: ${localeValue}`)\n  }\n  return templateDir\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Policy detection\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function findExternalPolicySources(root: string): string[] {\n  const matches = new Set<string>()\n\n  for (const pattern of ALWAYS_POLICY_SOURCE_PATTERNS) {\n    const p = join(root, pattern)\n    if (existsSync(p) && isFile(p)) {\n      matches.add(toPosixPath(relative(root, p)))\n    }\n  }\n\n  for (const pattern of CONTENT_POLICY_SOURCE_PATTERNS) {\n    const p = join(root, pattern)\n    if (existsSync(p) && isFile(p) && hasPolicyContent(p)) {\n      matches.add(toPosixPath(relative(root, p)))\n    }\n  }\n\n  return [...matches].sort()\n}\n\nexport function hasPolicyContent(filePath: string): boolean {\n  let text: string\n  try {\n    text = readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8').toLowerCase()\n  } catch {\n    return true\n  }\n  return POLICY_TEXT_PATTERNS.some(pattern => pattern.test(text))\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Version detection\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function findLatestVersion(folder: string, pattern: RegExp): string | null {\n  if (!existsSync(folder)) return null\n  const versions: string[] = []\n  try {\n    for (const entry of readdirSync(folder)) {\n      const match = pattern.exec(entry)\n      if (match?.[1]) {\n        versions.push(match[1])\n      }\n    }\n  } catch {\n    return null\n  }\n  return versions.length > 0 ? versions.sort().pop()! : null\n}\n\nexport function findLatestGoalFile(folder: string): string | null {\n  const regex = /^goal_v(\\d{3})_(\\d{8})\\.md$/\n  const matches: Array<[string, string, string]> = []\n  if (!existsSync(folder)) return null\n  try {\n    for (const entry of readdirSync(folder)) {\n      const match = regex.exec(entry)\n      if (match) {\n        matches.push([match[1]!, match[2]!, join(folder, entry)])\n      }\n    }\n  } catch {\n    return null\n  }\n  if (matches.length === 0) return null\n  matches.sort()\n  return matches[matches.length - 1]![2]\n}\n\nexport function findLatestTodoVersion(folder: string, goalVersion: string): string | null {\n  const regex = new RegExp(`^todo_v${goalVersion}_(\\\\d{3})_\\\\d{8}\\\\.md$`)\n  const versions: string[] = []\n  if (!existsSync(folder)) return null\n  try {\n    for (const entry of readdirSync(folder)) {\n      const match = regex.exec(entry)\n      if (match?.[1]) versions.push(match[1])\n    }\n  } catch {\n    return null\n  }\n  return versions.length > 0 ? versions.sort().pop()! : null\n}\n\nexport function findLatestTodoFile(folder: string, goalVersion: string): string | null {\n  const regex = new RegExp(`^todo_v${goalVersion}_(\\\\d{3})_(\\\\d{8})\\\\.md$`)\n  const matches: Array<[string, string, string]> = []\n  if (!existsSync(folder)) return null\n  try {\n    for (const entry of readdirSync(folder)) {\n      const match = regex.exec(entry)\n      if (match) matches.push([match[1]!, match[2]!, join(folder, entry)])\n    }\n  } catch {\n    return null\n  }\n  if (matches.length === 0) return null\n  matches.sort()\n  return matches[matches.length - 1]![2]\n}\n\nexport function findLatestChatLogFile(folder: string): string | null {\n  const regex = /^(\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2})_(\\d{2}-\\d{2})\\.md$/\n  const matches: Array<[string, string, string]> = []\n  if (!existsSync(folder)) return null\n  try {\n    for (const entry of readdirSync(folder)) {\n      const match = regex.exec(entry)\n      if (match) matches.push([match[1]!, match[2]!, join(folder, entry)])\n    }\n  } catch {\n    return null\n  }\n  if (matches.length === 0) return null\n  matches.sort()\n  return matches[matches.length - 1]![2]\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Template writing\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function writeTemplate(\n  templatePath: string,\n  targetPath: string,\n  force: boolean,\n  values: Record<string, string>,\n): boolean {\n  if (existsSync(targetPath) && !force) return false\n  let content = readFileSync(templatePath, 'utf-8')\n  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(values)) {\n    content = content.replaceAll(`{{${key}}}`, value)\n  }\n  mkdirSync(join(targetPath, '..'), { recursive: true })\n  writeFileSync(targetPath, content, 'utf-8')\n  return true\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Scaffold paths\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function resolveScaffoldPaths(root: string, dt: Date): ScaffoldPaths {\n  const y = dt.getFullYear()\n  const m = String(dt.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0')\n  const d = String(dt.getDate()).padStart(2, '0')\n  const hh = String(dt.getHours()).padStart(2, '0')\n  const mm = String(dt.getMinutes()).padStart(2, '0')\n\n  const dateToken = `${y}${m}${d}`\n  const dateLabel = `${y}-${m}-${d}`\n  const timeLabel = `${hh}:${mm}`\n  const chatFilename = `${y}-${m}-${d}_${hh}-${mm}.md`\n\n  const goalDir = join(root, 'Memory', '01_goals')\n  const todoDir = join(root, 'Memory', '02_todos')\n  const chatDir = join(root, 'Memory', '03_chat_logs')\n\n  let goalVersion = findLatestVersion(goalDir, /^goal_v(\\d{3})_\\d{8}\\.md$/)\n  if (goalVersion === null) goalVersion = '001'\n  const prevNum = parseInt(goalVersion, 10)\n  const previousVersion = prevNum > 1 ? `v${String(prevNum - 1).padStart(3, '0')}` : 'none'\n\n  let todoVersion = findLatestTodoVersion(todoDir, goalVersion)\n  if (todoVersion === null) todoVersion = '001'\n\n  const goalPath = findLatestGoalFile(goalDir) ?? join(goalDir, `goal_v${goalVersion}_${dateToken}.md`)\n  const todoPath = findLatestTodoFile(todoDir, goalVersion) ?? join(todoDir, `todo_v${goalVersion}_${todoVersion}_${dateToken}.md`)\n  const chatPath = findLatestChatLogFile(chatDir) ?? join(chatDir, chatFilename)\n\n  return {\n    root,\n    goalDir,\n    todoDir,\n    chatDir,\n    goalPath,\n    todoPath,\n    chatPath,\n    goalVersion,\n    todoVersion,\n    previousVersion,\n    dateLabel,\n    timeLabel,\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Directory creation\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function createMemoryDirs(root: string): string[] {\n  const created: string[] = []\n  for (const rel of MEMORY_DIRS) {\n    const p = join(root, rel)\n    if (!existsSync(p)) {\n      created.push(toPosixPath(rel))\n    }\n    mkdirSync(p, { recursive: true })\n  }\n  return created\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Content scaffolding\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function scaffoldContentFiles(\n  paths: ScaffoldPaths,\n  templates: string,\n  goalSummary: string,\n  projectName: string,\n  force: boolean,\n): { created: string[]; preserved: string[] } {\n  const created: string[] = []\n  const preserved: string[] = []\n\n  const pairs: Array<[string, string, Record<string, string>]> = [\n    [\n      join(templates, 'goal.md'),\n      paths.goalPath,\n      {\n        GOAL_VERSION: paths.goalVersion,\n        DATE: paths.dateLabel,\n        PREVIOUS_VERSION: paths.previousVersion,\n        GOAL_SUMMARY: goalSummary.trim(),\n      },\n    ],\n    [\n      join(templates, 'todo.md'),\n      paths.todoPath,\n      {\n        GOAL_VERSION: paths.goalVersion,\n        TODO_SUBVERSION: paths.todoVersion,\n        DATE: paths.dateLabel,\n      },\n    ],\n    [\n      join(templates, 'chat-log.md'),\n      paths.chatPath,\n      {\n        DATE: paths.dateLabel,\n        TIME: paths.timeLabel,\n        PROJECT_NAME: projectName.trim(),\n      },\n    ],\n  ]\n\n  for (const [templatePath, targetPath, values] of pairs) {\n    if (writeTemplate(templatePath, targetPath, force, values)) {\n      created.push(toPosixPath(relative(paths.root, targetPath)))\n    } else {\n      preserved.push(toPosixPath(relative(paths.root, targetPath)))\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { created, preserved }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Internal helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction isFile(p: string): boolean {\n  try { return require('node:fs').statSync(p).isFile() } catch { return false }\n}\n"
}

Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\src\\project\\upgrade.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * Upgrade — upgrade detection + execution.\n * Port of scripts/upgrade-memorytree.py\n */\n\nimport { existsSync, readFileSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport { join } from 'node:path'\n\nimport {\n  createMemoryDirs,\n  findExternalPolicySources,\n  findLatestChatLogFile,\n  findLatestGoalFile,\n  findLatestTodoFile,\n  resolveScaffoldPaths,\n  scaffoldContentFiles,\n  writeTemplate,\n} from './scaffold.js'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// State detection\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function detectState(root: string): string {\n  const memoryDir = join(root, 'Memory')\n  const goalDir = join(root, 'Memory', '01_goals')\n  const todoDir = join(root, 'Memory', '02_todos')\n  const chatDir = join(root, 'Memory', '03_chat_logs')\n  const agentsPath = join(root, 'AGENTS.md')\n  const policySources = findExternalPolicySources(root)\n\n  const latestGoal = findLatestGoalFile(goalDir)\n  const goalVersion = extractGoalVersion(latestGoal)\n  const hasGoal = latestGoal !== null\n  const hasTodo = goalVersion !== null && findLatestTodoFile(todoDir, goalVersion) !== null\n  const hasChat = findLatestChatLogFile(chatDir) !== null\n  const hasAgents = existsSync(agentsPath) && isFile(agentsPath)\n  const agentsReady = hasAgents && !needsAgentsMerge(agentsPath)\n\n  if (!existsSync(memoryDir)) {\n    return (hasAgents || policySources.length > 0) ? 'partial' : 'not-installed'\n  }\n  if (hasGoal && hasTodo && hasChat && agentsReady) {\n    return 'installed'\n  }\n  return 'partial'\n}\n\nexport function extractGoalVersion(filePath: string | null): string | null {\n  if (filePath === null) return null\n  const name = filePath.replace(/\\\\/g, '/').split('/').pop() ?? ''\n  const match = /^goal_v(\\d{3})_\\d{8}\\.md$/.exec(name)\n  return match?.[1] ?? null\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// AGENTS.md completeness check (9 signals)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport function needsAgentsMerge(agentsPath: string): boolean {\n  let text: string\n  try {\n    text = readFileSync(agentsPath, 'utf-8').toLowerCase()\n  } catch {\n    return true\n  }\n\n  const checks = [\n    hasReadOrderSignal(text),\n    hasMemoryLayoutSignal(text),\n    hasActiveTodoRule(text),\n    hasAppendOnlyChatLogRule(text),\n    hasRepoSafetyRule(text),\n    hasCommitTitleRule(text),\n    hasDedicatedMemorytreePrFlow(text),\n    hasMemorytreeOnlyScopeRule(text),\n    hasAutoMergeRule(text),\n  ]\n  return !checks.every(Boolean)\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Signal detectors\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction matchesAny(text: string, patterns: readonly RegExp[]): boolean {\n  return patterns.some(p => p.test(text))\n}\n\nfunction matchesAllConcepts(text: string, groups: readonly (readonly RegExp[])[]): boolean {\n  return groups.every(patterns => matchesAny(text, patterns))\n}\n\nexport function hasReadOrderSignal(text: string): boolean {\n  return matchesAny(text, [\n    /\\bread order\\b/, /\\bread .* in order\\b/, /读取顺序/, /按此顺序读取/,\n  ])\n}\n\nexport function hasMemoryLayoutSignal(text: string): boolean {\n  return matchesAllConcepts(text, [\n    [/memory\\/01_goals/, /\\b01_goals\\b/],\n    [/memory\\/02_todos/, /\\b02_todos\\b/],\n    [/memory\\/03_chat_logs/, /\\b03_chat_logs\\b/],\n  ])\n}\n\nexport function hasActiveTodoRule(text: string): boolean {\n  return matchesAny(text, [\n    /keep .*todo .*synchron/,\n    /todo .*aligned .*active goal/,\n    /(sync|align|keep|maintain|bind).{0,50}(active|current).{0,20}todo.{0,50}(active|current).{0,20}goal/,\n    /(active|current).{0,20}todo.{0,50}(sync|align|match|bound).{0,50}(active|current).{0,20}goal/,\n    /当前待办必须与当前目标保持同步/,\n    /待办必须与当前目标保持同步/,\n  ])\n}\n\nexport function hasAppendOnlyChatLogRule(text: string): boolean {\n  return matchesAny(text, [\n    /append[- ]only .*chat log/,\n    /append[- ]only records/,\n    /treat chat logs as append[- ]only/,\n    /never rewrite or delete prior entries/,\n    /对话日志只追加/,\n    /禁止重写或删除既有内容/,\n  ])\n}\n\nexport function hasRepoSafetyRule(text: string): boolean {\n  return matchesAllConcepts(text, [\n    [/\\bpr\\b/, /\\bpull request\\b/, /\\bmerge request\\b/, /拉取请求/, /合并请求/],\n    [/\\bci\\b/, /\\bchecks?\\b/, /\\bpipeline\\b/, /\\be2e\\b/, /检查/, /流水线/],\n    [/\\breview(?:er|ers)?\\b/, /\\bapproval(?:s)?\\b/, /评审/, /审批/],\n    [/\\bbranch(?:es)?\\b/, /\\bprotected branch(?:es)?\\b/, /分支/],\n  ]) && matchesAny(text, [\n    /\\brepository\\b/, /\\brepo\\b/, /\\bproject\\b/, /\\bhost\\b/,\n    /\\brule(?:s)?\\b/, /\\brequirement(?:s)?\\b/, /\\bpolicy\\b/, /\\bcontrol(?:s)?\\b/,\n    /仓库/, /规则/, /要求/,\n  ])\n}\n\nexport function hasCommitTitleRule(text: string): boolean {\n  return matchesAny(text, [\n    /\\bmemorytree\\([^)]*\\):/, /\\b[a-z]+\\((memorytree)\\):/,\n    /\\bmemorytree:/, /memorytree-scoped commit title/,\n    /memorytree-specific commit title/, /use .*memorytree.*commit title/,\n    /提交标题.*memorytree/,\n  ])\n}\n\nexport function hasDedicatedMemorytreePrFlow(text: string): boolean {\n  return matchesAllConcepts(text, [\n    [/\\bmemorytree\\b/, /记忆/],\n    [/\\bbranch(?:es)?\\b/, /分支/],\n    [/\\bpr\\b/, /\\bpull request\\b/, /\\bmerge request\\b/, /拉取请求/, /合并请求/],\n    [/\\bdedicated\\b/, /\\bseparate\\b/, /\\bisolated\\b/, /\\bown\\b/, /专用/, /独立/],\n  ])\n}\n\nexport function hasMemorytreeOnlyScopeRule(text: string): boolean {\n  return matchesAny(text, [\n    /memorytree[- ]owned changes/,\n    /memorytree[- ]owned files/,\n    /memorytree-managed files/,\n    /(stage|push|commit).{0,40}only.{0,40}memorytree[- ]owned.{0,20}(files|changes)?/,\n    /(stage|push|commit).{0,40}only.{0,40}memorytree.{0,20}(files|changes|diff)/,\n    /only.{0,40}memorytree.{0,20}(files|changes|diff)/,\n    /仅自动提交和推送.*memorytree.*变更/,\n    /由 memorytree 管理/,\n  ])\n}\n\nexport function hasAutoMergeRule(text: string): boolean {\n  return matchesAny(text, [\n    /auto-merge only when .*permit/,\n    /only when repository rules permit/,\n    /only when repo rules permit/,\n    /only enable auto-merge when.{0,60}(required )?(approvals|checks|reviews?)/,\n    /auto-merge.{0,60}(required approvals|required checks|repository rules)/,\n    /仅在仓库规则允许时才开启自动合并/,\n  ])\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Upgrade execution\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport interface UpgradeResult {\n  state_before: string\n  state_after: string\n  requested_locale: string\n  effective_locale: string\n  created_dirs: string[]\n  created_files: string[]\n  preserved_files: string[]\n  agents_action: string\n  agents_merge_required: boolean\n}\n\nexport function upgrade(\n  root: string,\n  skillRoot: string,\n  templates: string,\n  effectiveLocale: string,\n  requestedLocale: string,\n  goalSummary: string,\n  projectName: string,\n  dt: Date,\n): UpgradeResult {\n  const initialState = detectState(root)\n\n  const createdDirs = createMemoryDirs(root)\n  const paths = resolveScaffoldPaths(root, dt)\n  const { created: createdFiles, preserved: preservedFiles } = scaffoldContentFiles(\n    paths, templates, goalSummary, projectName, false,\n  )\n\n  const agentsPath = join(root, 'AGENTS.md')\n  let agentsAction = 'preserved_existing'\n  if (!existsSync(agentsPath)) {\n    const agentsTemplate = join(templates, 'agents.md')\n    if (existsSync(agentsTemplate) && writeTemplate(agentsTemplate, agentsPath, false, {})) {\n      createdFiles.push('AGENTS.md')\n      agentsAction = 'created'\n    }\n  } else {\n    preservedFiles.push('AGENTS.md')\n  }\n  const mergeRequired = agentsAction === 'preserved_existing' && needsAgentsMerge(agentsPath)\n\n  return {\n    state_before: initialState,\n    state_after: detectState(root),\n    requested_locale: requestedLocale,\n    effective_locale: effectiveLocale,\n    created_dirs: createdDirs,\n    created_files: createdFiles,\n    preserved_files: preservedFiles,\n    agents_action: agentsAction,\n    agents_merge_required: mergeRequired,\n  }\n}\n\nexport function formatResultText(result: UpgradeResult): string {\n  const lines = [\n    `state_before: ${result.state_before}`,\n    `state_after: ${result.state_after}`,\n    `effective_locale: ${result.effective_locale}`,\n    `agents_action: ${result.agents_action}`,\n    `agents_merge_required: ${result.agents_merge_required}`,\n  ]\n  if (result.created_dirs.length > 0) {\n    lines.push('created_dirs:')\n    for (const item of result.created_dirs) lines.push(`- ${item}`)\n  }\n  if (result.created_files.length > 0) {\n    lines.push('created_files:')\n    for (const item of result.created_files) lines.push(`- ${item}`)\n  }\n  if (result.preserved_files.length > 0) {\n    lines.push('preserved_files:')\n    for (const item of result.preserved_files) lines.push(`- ${item}`)\n  }\n  return lines.join('\\n')\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Internal helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction isFile(p: string): boolean {\n  try { return require('node:fs').statSync(p).isFile() } catch { return false }\n}\n"
}

  "old_string": "function readTextSample(filePath: string): string {\n  try {\n    const buf = Buffer.alloc(4096)\n    const fd = require('node:fs').openSync(filePath, 'r')\n    const bytesRead = require('node:fs').readSync(fd, buf, 0, 4096, 0)\n    require('node:fs').closeSync(fd)\n    return buf.subarray(0, bytesRead).toString('utf-8')\n  } catch {\n    return ''\n  }\n}",
  "new_string": "function readTextSample(filePath: string): string {\n  try {\n    const buf = Buffer.alloc(4096)\n    const fd = openSync(filePath, 'r')\n    const bytesRead = readSync(fd, buf, 0, 4096, 0)\n    closeSync(fd)\n    return buf.subarray(0, bytesRead).toString('utf-8')\n  } catch {\n    return ''\n  }\n}"
}

unknown passes through\n- `detectRepoLocale`: English repo (README.md with English text), Chinese repo (README.md with 中文), mixed repo, empty repo→null\n- `detectSystemLocale`: mock env vars (LC_ALL=zh_CN→'zh-cn', LANG=en_US→'en', no env→'en')\n\nFor detectRepoLocale, create temp dirs with README.md files:\n- English: write a README with ~100 English words\n- Chinese: write a README with created list, idempotent (second call returns empty)\n- `resolveScaffoldPaths`: returns correct paths with version defaults (001), finds existing versions\n- `findLatestVersion`: with matches, without matches, empty dir\n- `findLatestGoalFile`: finds latest by version+date, returns null for empty\n- `findLatestTodoFile`: finds matching todo for goal version\n- `findLatestChatLogFile`: finds latest by date+time\n- `writeTemplate`: writes with placeholder substitution, skips existing (force=false), overwrites (force=true)\n- `scaffoldContentFiles`: creates goal+todo+chat files from templates, preserves existing\n- `hasPolicyContent`: detects policy text, returns false for non-policy\n- `findExternalPolicySources`: finds CODEOWNERS, CONTRIBUTING.md etc.\n\nFor template tests, create temp dirs with template files containing `{{KEY}}` placeholders.\n\n### upgrade.test.ts (~15 tests):\nRead the source: `D:\\demo1\\memorytree-workflow\\src\\project\\upgrade.ts`\n\nTest functions:\n- `detectState`: 'not-installed' (empty repo), 'partial' (has AGENTS.md but no Memory/), 'installed' (full setup)\n- `extractGoalVersion`: extracts from filename, returns null for non-matching\n- text\n- `upgrade`: end-to-end with temp dir (requires template files)\n- `formatResultText`: formats output correctly\n\nFor the signal detector tests, construct text strings that match/don't match <summary>Agent "Write Phase 3 tests" completed</summary>
<result>All 90 tests pass. Here is a summary of what was created:

Files created:

1. D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\tests\project\locale.test.ts -- 18 tests
- normalizeLocale (7 tests): direct mappings (en, zh-cn, zh, en-us, zh-tw), auto-detect with root dir, unknown passthrough
- detectRepoLocale (6 tests): English README, Chinese README, mixed/balanced content, empty repo, non-existent dir, zh-CN filename detection
- detectSystemLocale (5 tests): LC_ALL=zh_CN, LANG=en_US, no env vars, LANGUAGE=Chinese, LC_ALL priority over LANG

2. D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\tests\project\scaffold.test.ts -- 35 tests
- buildDatetime (4 tests): date+time, date only, time only, neither
- createMemoryDirs (3 tests): creates 5 dirs, posix format paths, idempotent
- resolveScaffoldPaths (2 tests): default version 001, finds existing versions
- findLatestVersion (3 tests): highest version, no matches, non-existent dir
- findLatestGoalFile (3 tests): latest by version+date, empty dir, non-existent dir
- findLatestTodoFile (2 tests): matching goal version, no matches
- findLatestChatLogFile (3 tests): latest by date+time, empty dir, non-existent dir
- writeTemplate (3 tests): placeholder substitution, skip existing (force=false), overwrite (force=true)
- scaffoldContentFiles (2 tests): creates goal+todo+chat, preserves existing
- hasPolicyContent (4 tests): pull request keyword, conventional commits, non-policy, Chinese terms
- findExternalPolicySources (6 tests): CODEOWNERS, .github/CODEOWNERS, CONTRIBUTING.md with/without policy content, empty repo, sorted results

3. D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\tests\project\upgrade.test.ts -- 37 tests
- detectState (4 tests): not-installed, partial (AGENTS.md only), installed (full setup), partial (Memory without files)
- extractGoalVersion (4 tests): valid path, Windows path, non-matching, null input
- needsAgentsMerge (3 tests): incomplete, complete with all 9 signals, non-existent
- Signal detectors (18 tests, 2-3 per detector): hasReadOrderSignal, hasMemoryLayoutSignal, hasActiveTodoRule, hasAppendOnlyChatLogRule, hasRepoSafetyRule, hasCommitTitleRule, hasDedicatedMemorytreePrFlow, hasMemorytreeOnlyScopeRule, hasAutoMergeRule -- each with positive and negative cases
- upgrade (2 tests): full e2e on empty dir, preserves on re-run
- formatResultText (2 tests): with created items, with empty lists

All tests use mkdtempSync for temp dirs, clean up in afterEach with rmSync, import from vitest, and use .js extensions in imports.</result>
